title.zh: 卷五十一 李陳龐陳橋列傳
title.en: Volume 51: Biographies of Li, Chen, Pang, Chen, Qiao
title.raw: 卷五十一 李陳龐陳橋列傳 Volume 51: Biographies of Li, Chen, Pang, Chen, Qiao

sentences.zh: 李恂字叔英，安定臨涇人也。

translations.literal: Li Xun, courtesy name Shuying, was a man of Linying in Anding.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xun, courtesy name Shuying, came from Linying in Anding commandery.

sentences.zh: 少習韓詩，[一]教授諸生常數百人。

translations.literal: In youth he studied the Han version of the Odes; [one] he instructed students, always several hundred.
translations.idiomatic: He mastered the Han school Odes in his youth and regularly taught several hundred pupils.

sentences.zh: 太守穎川李鴻請署功曹，未及到，而州闢為從事。

translations.literal: Grand Warden Li Hong of Yingchuan requested him to take acting duty as merit assessor; before he arrived, the province summoned him as an adjutant.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Warden Li Hong of Yingchuan wanted him as acting merit assessor, but the provincial office beat him to it with an adjutant appointment.

sentences.zh: 會鴻卒，恂不應州命，而送鴻喪還鄉里。

translations.literal: When Hong died, Xun did not answer the province's summons but escorted Hong's funeral back to his home.
translations.idiomatic: When Li Hong died, Li Xun ignored the provincial call and accompanied the coffin to Li's home.

sentences.zh: 既葬，留起頤墳，持喪三年。

translations.literal: After burial, he remained to raise a grave mound at the chin and observed mourning three years.
translations.idiomatic: After the burial he stayed to build a memorial tumulus and kept the full three years of mourning.

sentences.zh: 注[一]韓嬰所傳詩也。

translations.literal: Comment one: It is the Odes transmitted by Han Ying.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: The text means the Han Ying recension of the Classic of Odes.

sentences.zh: 辟司徒桓虞府。

translations.literal: He was summoned to the office of Minister over the Masses Huan Yu.
translations.idiomatic: He received a staff appointment under Minister over the Masses Huan Yu.

sentences.zh: 後拜侍御史，持節使幽州，宣佈恩澤，慰撫北狄，所過皆圖寫山川、屯田、聚落百餘卷，悉封奏上，肅宗嘉之。

translations.literal: Later he was appointed palace attendant censor, bearing credentials he was sent to Youzhou, proclaimed grace and favor, comforted the northern Di; everywhere he passed he drew maps and wrote up mountains and rivers, garrison farms, and settlements in more than a hundred scrolls, all sealed and memorialized upward; Suzong praised him.
translations.idiomatic: Promoted to palace censor, he toured Youzhou with imperial credentials, reassuring the northern tribes while compiling illustrated gazetteers of terrain, military colonies, and settlements in over a hundred fascicles for the throne. Emperor Zhang commended him.

sentences.zh: 拜兗州刺史。

translations.literal: He was appointed governor of Yan province.
translations.idiomatic: He was named governor of Yan province.

sentences.zh: 以清約率下，常席羊皮，服布被。

translations.literal: With purity and frugality he led those below; he regularly sat on sheepskin and wore a cloth quilt.
translations.idiomatic: He led by austere example, sitting on a sheepskin mat and sleeping under a homespun quilt.

sentences.zh: 遷張掖太守，有威重名。

translations.literal: He was transferred to grand warden of Zhangye and had a name for awe-inspiring dignity.
translations.idiomatic: As grand warden of Zhangye he earned a reputation for stern integrity.

sentences.zh: 時大將軍竇憲將兵屯武威，天下州郡遠近莫不修禮遺，恂奉公不阿，為憲所奏免。

translations.literal: At the time Grand General Dou Xian led troops stationed at Wuwei; near and far no commandery or kingdom failed to send ritual gifts, but Xun upheld public duty without flattery and was memorialized by Xian and dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: When Dou Xian quartered the army at Wuwei, every locality sent him presents; Li Xun refused to curry favor and Dou Xian had him impeached and removed.

sentences.zh: 後復征拜謁者，使持節領西域副校尉。

translations.literal: Later he was again summoned and appointed herald, bearing credentials as acting deputy protector of the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: He was later recalled as herald with credentials to serve as acting deputy protector-general of the Western Regions.

sentences.zh: 西域殷富，多珍寶，諸國侍子及督使賈胡[一]數遺恂奴婢、宛馬、金銀、香罽之屬，一無所受。

translations.literal: The Western Regions were rich and had many treasures; the hostage princes of the various states and overseers and Hu merchants [one] repeatedly sent Xun slave-girls, Ferghana horses, gold and silver, scented woolens, and the like; he accepted none.
translations.idiomatic: The Western Regions teemed with wealth; princes' hostages, envoys, and Sogdian traders pressed gifts of slaves, horses, bullion, and fine carpets on him, and he turned every one away.

sentences.zh: [二]北匈奴數斷西域車師、伊吾，隴沙以西使命不得通，[三]恂設購賞，遂斬虜帥，縣首軍門。

translations.literal: [Two] The Northern Xiongnu repeatedly cut the Western Regions at Jushi and Yiwu; west of Longsand missions could not pass; [three] Xun set a reward price, then beheaded the bandit leader and hung the head at the camp gate.
translations.idiomatic: Northern Xiongnu raids severed the route through Jushi and Yiwu; west of the Longsand deserts couriers could not get through. Li Xun posted a bounty, slew the raiders' chief, and displayed the head at his headquarters.

sentences.zh: 自是道路夷清，威恩並行。

translations.literal: From this the roads were level and clear, awe and grace went together.
translations.idiomatic: After that the roads were safe and his authority and kindness were felt alike.

sentences.zh: 注[一]督使，主蕃國之使也。

translations.literal: Comment one: Overseer means one who directs the states' envoys.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: An overseer supervised tributary embassies from the oasis kingdoms.

sentences.zh: 賈胡，胡之商賈也。

translations.literal: Hu merchants means merchants of the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: The phrase means foreign traders from the steppe and oases.

sentences.zh: 注[二]袁山松書曰：「西域出諸香、石蜜。

translations.literal: Comment two: Yuan Shansong's book says: "The Western Regions produce various aromatics and rock honey.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Shansong records that the region yielded costly incense and candied sugar.

sentences.zh: 」罽，織毛為布者。

translations.literal: " Ji means one who weaves wool into cloth.
translations.idiomatic: Ji denotes woolen pile carpets.

sentences.zh: 注[三]前書曰：「車師前國王居交河城。

translations.literal: Comment three: The Former Documents says: "The king of Front Jushi dwelt at Jiaohe city.
translations.idiomatic: Note 3: The Han shu places the king of Front Jushi at Jiaohe.

sentences.zh: 」伊吾故城在今瓜州晉昌縣北。

translations.literal: " The old city of Yiwu is north of Jinchang County in Guazhou today.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient Yiwu lay north of present Jinchang in Gansu.

sentences.zh: 廣志曰：「流沙在玉門關外，東西數百里，有三斷名曰三隴也。」

translations.literal: Guangzhi says: "The flowing sands lie outside Jade Gate, several hundred li east to west; there are three breaks called Three Longs.
translations.idiomatic: The Guangzhi describes the desert belt beyond Yumenguan as the Three Longs.

sentences.zh: 遷武威太守。

translations.literal: He was transferred to grand warden of Wuwei.
translations.idiomatic: He was promoted to grand warden of Wuwei.

sentences.zh: 後坐事免，步歸鄉里，潛居山澤，結草為廬，獨與諸生織席自給。

translations.literal: Later for an affair he was dismissed, walked back to his home village, dwelt hidden in mountains and marshes, tied grass for a hut, alone with students wove mats to supply themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Later removed for an offense, he walked home, hid in the hills, thatched a grass hut, and wove reed mats with his disciples to live.

sentences.zh: 會西羌反畔，恂到田舍，為所執獲。

translations.literal: When the western Qiang rebelled, Xun arrived at a field hut and was seized.
translations.idiomatic: When the western Qiang rose, they captured him at a farmstead.

sentences.zh: 羌素聞其名，放遣之。

translations.literal: The Qiang had long heard his name and released him.
translations.idiomatic: The tribesmen knew his reputation and let him go.

sentences.zh: 恂因詣洛陽謝。

translations.literal: Xun therefore went to Luoyang to give thanks.
translations.idiomatic: He then traveled to Luoyang to report his release.

sentences.zh: 時歲荒，司空張敏、司徒魯恭等各遣子饋糧，悉無所受。

translations.literal: At the time the year was famine; Minister of Works Zhang Min and Minister over the Masses Lu Gong each sent their sons with grain; he accepted none.
translations.idiomatic: In a year of dearth, Zhang Min and Lu Gong each sent their sons with food; he refused it all.

sentences.zh: 徙居新安關下，拾橡實以自資。

translations.literal: He moved to dwell below the Xin'an pass, gathering acorns to supply himself.
translations.idiomatic: He moved below the Xin'an pass and lived on gathered acorns.

sentences.zh: [一]年九十六卒。

translations.literal: [One] At age ninety-six he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died at ninety-six.

sentences.zh: 注[一]橡，櫟實也。

translations.literal: Comment one: Acorn is the fruit of the oak.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: The text means acorns from oak trees.

sentences.zh: 武帝元鼎三年徙函谷關於新安也。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yuanding of Emperor Wu the Hangu pass was moved to Xin'an.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu had shifted the Hangu barrier to Xin'an in 114 BCE.

sentences.zh: 陳禪字紀山，巴郡安漢人也。

translations.literal: Chen Chan, courtesy name Jishan, was a man of Anhan in Ba commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Chan, courtesy name Jishan, came from Anhan in Ba commandery.

sentences.zh: 仕郡功曹，舉善黜惡，為邦內所畏。

translations.literal: He served the commandery as merit assessor, promoting good and demoting evil, feared within the state.
translations.idiomatic: As commandery merit assessor he promoted the worthy and ousted the corrupt, and the whole region stood in awe of him.

sentences.zh: 察孝廉，州辟治中從事。

translations.literal: Recommended filial and incorrupt, the province summoned him as administrative adjutant.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as filial and incorrupt, he was called up as the province's administrative adjutant.

sentences.zh: [一]時刺史為人所上受納臧賂，禪當傳考，[二]無它所繼，但持喪斂之具而已。

translations.literal: [One] At the time the governor was reported by someone for accepting bribes; Chan was to be arrested and examined; [two] he had no other luggage but brought only funeral shroud gear.
translations.idiomatic: When the governor was impeached for graft, Chen Chan was summoned as a witness; he arrived carrying nothing but a shroud for his own burial.

sentences.zh: 及至，笞掠無筭，五毒畢加，禪神意自若，辭對無變，事遂散釋。

translations.literal: When he arrived, flogging without count, the five poisons all applied, Chan's spirit and bearing were calm, replies without change, and the affair was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: They tortured him without limit, yet he remained composed and his story never wavered, so the case collapsed.

sentences.zh: 車騎將軍鄧騭聞其名而辟焉，舉茂才。

translations.literal: General of Chariots and Cavalry Deng Zhi heard his name and summoned him, recommending him as abundant talent.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Zhi, general of chariots and cavalry, recruited him and nominated him as abundant talent.

sentences.zh: 時漢中蠻夷反畔，以禪為漢中太守。

translations.literal: At the time Hanzhong barbarians rebelled; Chan was made grand warden of Hanzhong.
translations.idiomatic: When the Hanzhong tribes revolted, the court made him grand warden there.

sentences.zh: 夷賊素聞其聲，實時降服。

translations.literal: The barbarian bandits had long heard his reputation and at once submitted.
translations.idiomatic: The rebels had heard of him and surrendered immediately.

sentences.zh: 遷左馮翊，入拜諫議大夫。

translations.literal: He was transferred to Administrator of the Left, entered and was appointed grandee remonstrant.
translations.idiomatic: He rose to governor of the capital left and then entered court as grandee remonstrant.

sentences.zh: 注[一]續漢志曰，每州有持中從事也。

translations.literal: Comment one: The Continued Han Treatise says each province had a "holding center" adjutant.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: The Hou Han treatise lists a provincial adjutant for each circuit; some editions corrupt the title to read holding-center adjutant instead of administrative adjutant.

sentences.zh: 注[二]傳謂逮捕而考之也。

translations.literal: Comment two: "Transmit" here means arrest and examine.
translations.idiomatic: Note 2: "To transmit" in this context means to take into custody for questioning.

sentences.zh: 單于懷服，遺以胡中珍貨而去。

translations.literal: The Shanyu was moved to submission, presented him with precious goods from the Hu domain, and departed.
translations.idiomatic: The Shanyu submitted, left rare gifts from the steppe, and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 注[一]撣音徒丹反。

translations.literal: Comment one: Shan is read with the tu-dan fanqie spelling.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: The graph for the kingdom name Shan is glossed with the tu-dan fanqie reading.

sentences.zh: 注[二]家語曰，魯定公與齊侯會於夾谷，孔子攝相事。

translations.literal: Comment two: The Family Sayings says: Duke Ding of Lu met the marquis of Qi at Jiagu, and Kongzi acted as master of ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: Note 2: The Kongzi jiayu relates the Jiagu conference where Confucius directed the ritual.

sentences.zh: 齊奏中宮之樂，倡優侏儒戲於前。

translations.literal: Qi presented music of the inner palace, performers and dwarfs played before them.
translations.idiomatic: The Qi side staged palace entertainers and dwarf actors.

sentences.zh: 孔子趨曰：「匹夫而侮諸侯，罪應誅。

translations.literal: Kongzi stepped forward saying: "A commoner who insults the feudal lords deserves execution.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius protested: "To humiliate the lords of the land is a capital crime.

sentences.zh: 」於是斬侏儒，手足異處。

translations.literal: Thereupon they beheaded the dwarf, hands and feet in different places.
translations.idiomatic: They executed the dwarf and displayed the severed limbs.

sentences.zh: 注[三]論語孔子之言。

translations.literal: Comment three: A saying of Confucius in the Analects.
translations.idiomatic: Note 3: The line echoes the Analects.

sentences.zh: 注[四]詩小雅鼓鐘之詩曰：「以雅以南，以鑰不僭。

translations.literal: Comment four: The "Minor Odes" poem "Drum and Bell" says: "With ya and with nan, with key without presumption.
translations.idiomatic: Note 4: The Book of Odes praises proper use of ya and southern music.

sentences.zh: 」薛君云：「南夷之樂曰南。

translations.literal: " Xue Jun says: "The music of the southern Yi is called nan.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Han explains that southern tribal music was called nan.

sentences.zh: 四夷之樂唯南可以和於雅者，以其人聲音及鑰不僭差也。

translations.literal: Of the four Yi musics only nan could harmonize with ya, because their people's voices and keys did not stray.
translations.idiomatic: Only the southern style could blend with classical ya because its pitch stayed true.

sentences.zh: 」周禮，鞮鞻氏掌四夷之樂。

translations.literal: " The Zhou li: the Di-lou office masters the four Yi musics.
translations.idiomatic: The Rites of Zhou assigns foreign performances to the Di-lou.

sentences.zh: 鄭玄注云：「東方曰韎，南方曰任，西方曰朱離，北方曰禁。

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan's commentary says: "The east is called Mei, the south Ren, the west Zhuli, the north Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan names the four regional styles east, south, west, and north.

sentences.zh: 」毛詩無「韎任朱離」之文，蓋見齊、魯之詩也，今亡。

translations.literal: " The Mao Odes lack the graphs "Mei Ren Zhuli"; probably they appeared in the Qi and Lu recensions, now lost.
translations.idiomatic: Those graphs do not appear in the Mao recension, perhaps they survived only in Qi and Lu texts now lost.

sentences.zh: 韎音昧。

translations.literal: Mei is read like mei.
translations.idiomatic: The graph mei is read with the mei gloss.

sentences.zh: 禮記曰，九夷、八蠻、六戎、五狄來朝，立於明堂四門之外也。

translations.literal: The Record of Rites says the nine Yi, eight Man, six Rong, and five Di came to court and stood outside the four gates of the Bright Hall.
translations.idiomatic: The Liji states that outer tribes attended audience outside the Bright Hall's four gates.

sentences.zh: 注[五]前書西域傳曰：「縣度者，山名也。

translations.literal: Comment five: The Former Documents treatise on the Western Regions says: "Suspension Crossing is a mountain name.
translations.idiomatic: Note 5: The Han shu describes Suspension Crossing as a mountain range.

sentences.zh: 溪谷不通，以繩索相引而度，去陽關五千八百八十里。」

translations.literal: Ravines did not connect; they crossed by ropes linked together; distance from Yang Pass five thousand eight hundred eighty li.
translations.idiomatic: Travelers crossed on rope bridges; it lay thousands of li west of Yang Pass.

sentences.zh: 注[六]訕，謗也。

translations.literal: Comment six: Shan means slander.
translations.idiomatic: Note 6: Shan here means to defame.

sentences.zh: 注[七]候城，縣，在遼東。

translations.literal: Comment seven: Houcheng is a county in Liaodong.
translations.idiomatic: Note 7: Houcheng County lay in Liaodong commandery.

sentences.zh: 及鄧騭誅廢，禪以故吏免。

translations.literal: When Deng Zhi was executed and deposed, Chan as a former subordinate was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: When Deng Zhi fell, Chen Chan lost his post as a former client.

sentences.zh: 復為車騎將軍閻顯長史。

translations.literal: Again he became chief clerk to General of Chariots and Cavalry Yan Xian.
translations.idiomatic: He later served Yan Xian as chief clerk of the same general's command.

sentences.zh: 順帝即位，遷司隸校尉。

translations.literal: When Emperor Shun succeeded, he was transferred to colonel director of retainers.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Shun promoted him to colonel director of retainers.

sentences.zh: 明年，卒於官。

translations.literal: The next year he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office the following year.

sentences.zh: 子澄，有清名，官至漢中太守。

translations.literal: His son Cheng had a name for purity and reached office as grand warden of Hanzhong.
translations.idiomatic: His son Chen Cheng was known for integrity and rose to grand warden of Hanzhong.

sentences.zh: 禪曾孫寶，亦剛壯有禪風，為州別駕從事，顯名州裡。

translations.literal: Chan's great-grandson Bao was also firm and bold with Chan's manner, served as provincial aide-de-camp, and was noted in the province.
translations.idiomatic: His great-grandson Chen Bao inherited his toughness, served as provincial aide-de-camp, and made a name locally.

sentences.zh: 龐參字仲達，河南緱氏人也。

translations.literal: Pang Shen, courtesy name Zhongda, was a man of Goushi in Henan.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Shen, courtesy name Zhongda, came from Goushi in Henan.

sentences.zh: 初仕郡，未知名，河南尹龐奮見而奇之，舉為孝廉，拜左校令。

translations.literal: At first he served the commandery without fame; the Henan governor Pang Fen saw him and thought him extraordinary, recommended him filial and incorrupt, appointed him colonel of the left watch.
translations.idiomatic: He began in obscurity until Governor Pang Fen singled him out, nominated him filial and incorrupt, and made him colonel of the left watch.

sentences.zh: 坐法輸作若盧。

translations.literal: For violating the law he was sentenced to labor at Ruolu.
translations.idiomatic: A legal conviction sent him to corvée labor at Ruolu prison workshop.

sentences.zh: [一] 注[一]若盧，獄名。

translations.literal: [One] Comment one: Ruolu is a prison name.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: Ruolu was the name of an imperial prison workshop.

sentences.zh: 永初元年，涼州先零種羌反畔，遣車騎將軍鄧騭討之。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongchu the Qiang of Xianling type in Liangzhou rebelled; General of Chariots and Cavalry Deng Zhi was sent to punish them.
translations.idiomatic: In 107 CE the Xianling Qiang of Liangzhou rose; Deng Zhi marched against them.

sentences.zh: 參於徒中使其子俊上書曰：「方今西州流民擾動，而征發不絕，水潦不休，地力不復。

translations.literal: From among the convicts Shen had his son Jun submit a memorial saying: "At present the western province's wandering people are in turmoil, yet levies do not cease, floods do not stop, the land's strength does not recover.
translations.idiomatic: From his labor gang Pang Shen had his son memorialize: "The west is in uproar, yet conscription never stops, floods keep coming, and the soil cannot recover.

sentences.zh: [一]重之以大軍，疲之以遠戍，農功消於轉運，資財竭於征發。

translations.literal: [One] Add heavy armies, exhaust them with distant garrison duty; farming achievement vanishes in transport, resources are drained by levies.
translations.idiomatic: Mass armies and distant garrisons waste the harvest in cartage and bleed the treasury dry.

sentences.zh: 田疇不得墾闢，禾稼不得收入，搏手困窮，無望來秋。

translations.literal: Fields cannot be opened, grain cannot be gathered; wringing empty hands in destitution, there is no hope for next autumn.
translations.idiomatic: Fields lie untilled and grain ungathered; the people wring their hands in poverty with no hope for the next harvest.

sentences.zh: [二]百姓力屈，不復堪命。

translations.literal: [Two] The common people's strength is bent; they can no longer bear the command.
translations.idiomatic: The commoners are at the breaking point and cannot obey another order.

sentences.zh: 臣愚以為萬里運糧，遠就羌戎，不若總兵養觿，以待其疲。

translations.literal: Your subject foolishly thinks that hauling grain ten thousand li to join battle far among the Qiang is not so good as gathering troops and nurturing the masses to await their exhaustion.
translations.idiomatic: Better to husband strength and wait the enemy out than to ship grain thousands of li to fight the Qiang in their hills.

sentences.zh: 車騎將軍騭宜且振旅，留征西校尉任尚使督涼州士民，轉居三輔。

translations.literal: General of Chariots and Cavalry Zhi should for the moment withdraw the army, leave Colonel on the West Ren Shang to supervise Liangzhou gentry and commoners, and shift them to settle in the three adjuncts.
translations.idiomatic: Let Deng Zhi pull back and leave Colonel Ren Shang to shepherd Liangzhou people into the three capital districts.

sentences.zh: 休徭役以助其時，止煩賦以益其財，令男得耕種，女得織□，[三]然後畜精銳，乘懈沮，出其不意。

translations.literal: Rest corvée to help their seasons, stop vexing taxes to increase their wealth, order men able to plow and women able to weave (one character missing in received text); [three] then store sharp troops, strike slackness and despair, catch them unawares.
translations.idiomatic: Suspend labor service, cut exorbitant levies, let men farm and women weave (lacuna in text), then strike when the tribes relax.

sentences.zh: 攻其不備，則邊人之仇報，奔北之恥雪矣。

translations.literal: Attack where they are unprepared, then the border people's vengeance will be repaid and the shame of rout wiped away.
translations.idiomatic: A surprise blow will avenge the frontier and wipe away the disgrace of defeat.

sentences.zh: 」書奏，會御史中丞樊准上疏薦參曰：「臣聞鷙鳥累百，不如一鶚。

translations.literal: " When the memorial was submitted, it happened that imperial secretary Fan Zhun submitted a memorial recommending Shen saying: "Your subject has heard that a hundred fierce birds are not worth one osprey.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial arrived as Fan Zhun urged: "A hundred kites are not worth one eagle.

sentences.zh: [四] 昔孝文皇帝悟馮唐之言，而赦魏尚之罪，使為邊守，匈奴不敢南向。

translations.literal: [Four] Formerly Emperor Wen understood Feng Tang's words, pardoned Wei Shang's crime, made him border guardian, and the Xiongnu did not dare face south.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen heeded Feng Tang and restored Wei Shang; the Xiongnu dared not raid the south.

sentences.zh: [五]夫以一臣之身，折方面之難者，選用得也。

translations.literal: [Five] That one minister's person could break a regional crisis shows the right choice of men.
translations.idiomatic: One good appointment can secure a border.

sentences.zh: 臣伏見故左校令河南龐參，勇謀不測，卓爾奇偉，高才武略，有魏尚之風。

translations.literal: Your subject has seen the former colonel of the left watch Pang Shen of Henan: courage and counsel unfathomable, outstanding and extraordinary, high talent and martial strategy, with Wei Shang's manner.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Shen of Henan has Wei Shang's mettle—bold, shrewd, and fit for the field.

sentences.zh: 前坐微法，輸作經時。

translations.literal: Earlier for a minor statute he was sentenced to labor for a long time.
translations.idiomatic: A small offense once sent him to the labor gangs.

sentences.zh: 今羌戎為患，大軍西屯，臣以為如參之人，宜在行伍。

translations.literal: Now the Qiang are the trouble and a great army camps in the west; your subject thinks a man like Shen belongs in the ranks.
translations.idiomatic: With the army pinned in the west, men like him belong in uniform.

sentences.zh: 惟明詔采前世之舉，觀魏尚之功，免赦參刑，以為軍鋒，必有成效，宣助國威。

translations.literal: May an enlightened edict take a precedent from past promotions, look on Wei Shang's merit, pardon Shen's sentence, and make him vanguard of the army; there will surely be achievement and the state's majesty will be proclaimed.
translations.idiomatic: Pardon him and put him in the van and you will stiffen the empire's arm.

sentences.zh: 」鄧太后納其言，即擢參於徒中，召拜謁者，使西督三輔諸軍屯，而征鄧騭還。

translations.literal: " Empress Deng accepted the words, at once raised Shen from among convicts, summoned and appointed him herald, sent him west to oversee the three adjuncts' garrison camps, and recalled Deng Zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Deng agreed: she freed Pang Shen, made him a herald to supervise western camps, and recalled Deng Zhi.

sentences.zh: 注[一]言其耗損，不復於舊。

translations.literal: Comment one: It speaks of depletion, not returning to the old.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: Meaning the land's strength was spent.

sentences.zh: 注[二]兩手相搏，言無計也。

translations.literal: Comment two: Two hands wringing each other means no plan.
translations.idiomatic: Note 2: Clasped empty hands mean helplessness.

sentences.zh: 注[三]□音如深反。

translations.literal: Comment three: The lacuna graph is read with the ru-shen fanqie spelling.
translations.idiomatic: Note 3: The damaged character is glossed ru-shen.

sentences.zh: 杜預注左傳云：「織□，織繒布也。」

translations.literal: Du Yu's commentary on the Zuo says: "Weaving (lacuna) means weaving tabby silk.
translations.idiomatic: Du Yu glosses the phrase as weaving silk tabby.

sentences.zh: 注[四]前書鄒陽諫吳王之辭也。

translations.literal: Comment four: A line from Zou Yang's remonstrance to the king of Wu in the Former Documents.
translations.idiomatic: Note 4: The osprey image comes from Zou Yang's memorial in the Han shu.

sentences.zh: 鶚，大鵰也。

translations.literal: The osprey is a great eagle.
translations.idiomatic: An osprey is a great sea eagle.

sentences.zh: 注[五]前書馮唐謂文帝曰：「臣聞魏尚為雲中守，匈奴遠避，不近雲中之塞。

translations.literal: Comment five: The Former Documents: Feng Tang told Emperor Wen: "Your subject has heard that Wei Shang as guardian of Yunzhong kept the Xiongnu far away and not near Yunzhong's barrier.
translations.idiomatic: Note 5: Feng Tang's plea recalled Wei Shang's success on the northern frontier.

sentences.zh: 上功莫府，一言不相應，文吏以法繩之。

translations.literal: He reported merit to the headquarters; one word did not match; clerks applied the statutes to him.
translations.idiomatic: Petty clerks faulted him over a single discrepancy in his battle report.

sentences.zh: 愚以為陛下法太明而賞太輕。

translations.literal: Your subject foolishly thinks Your Majesty's laws are too clear and rewards too light.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Tang warned that harsh law and stingy reward would drive good generals away.

sentences.zh: 」文帝悅，是日令唐持節赦魏尚，復以為雲中守也。

translations.literal: " Emperor Wen was pleased; that day he ordered Tang bearing credentials to pardon Wei Shang and again made him guardian of Yunzhong.
translations.idiomatic: Wen approved and sent Feng Tang with credentials to restore Wei Shang that same day.

sentences.zh: 四年，羌寇轉盛，兵費日廣，且連年不登，谷石萬餘。

translations.literal: In the fourth year the Qiang raids grew worse, military costs daily broader, and moreover years in a row had no harvest, grain a stone over ten thousand.
translations.idiomatic: By the fourth year the Qiang war was bleeding the treasury dry; harvests had failed year after year and grain cost more than ten thousand cash a bushel.

sentences.zh: 參奏記於鄧騭曰：「比年羌寇特困隴右，供徭賦役為損日滋，官負人責數十億萬。

translations.literal: Shen submitted a memorial to Deng Zhi saying: "In recent years the Qiang raids have especially exhausted Longyou; supply for corvée, tax, and labor daily increases in loss; the government owes private debts in the tens of billions.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Shen wrote Deng Zhi: "Longyou is being bled white; every levy swells the deficit until the state owes tens of billions.

sentences.zh: [一]今復募發百姓，調取谷帛，衒賣什物，以應吏求。

translations.literal: [One] Now again conscripting the common people, requisitioning grain and silk, hawking goods to meet clerks' demands.
translations.idiomatic: The court keeps drafting peasants, seizing grain and cloth, and forcing families to sell their goods to satisfy petty officials.

sentences.zh: 外傷羌虜，內困徵賦。

translations.literal: Outwardly wounded by Qiang bandits, inwardly strangled by levies.
translations.idiomatic: The people suffer the raiders without and the taxman within.

sentences.zh: [二]遂乃千里轉糧，遠給武都西郡。

translations.literal: [Two] Then they haul grain a thousand li to supply Wudu and the western commanderies afar.
translations.idiomatic: Grain trains crawl a thousand li to feed distant Wudu and the western frontier.

sentences.zh: 塗路傾阻，難勞百端，疾行則鈔暴為害，遲進則谷食稍損，運糧散於曠野，牛馬死於山澤。

translations.literal: The roads are steep and blocked, hardships and toils of every kind; hurry and raiders plunder; dawdle and the grain slowly spoils; transport grain scatters on the open plain, oxen and horses die in hills and marshes.
translations.idiomatic: The routes are murder: march fast and convoys are looted; march slow and the grain rots; supplies rot in the wilderness and draft animals drop dead.

sentences.zh: 縣官不足，輒貸於民。

translations.literal: When the county treasury is insufficient, they borrow from the people.
translations.idiomatic: When county granaries run dry, officials borrow from the peasants they already ruined.

sentences.zh: 民已窮矣，將從誰求？

translations.literal: The people are already destitute; from whom will they seek?
translations.idiomatic: The people are broke—who is left to squeeze?

sentences.zh: 名救金城，而實困三輔。

translations.literal: In name they rescue Jincheng, but in fact they exhaust the three adjuncts.
translations.idiomatic: The policy pretends to save Jincheng while it beggars the capital region.

sentences.zh: 三輔既困，還復為金城之禍矣。

translations.literal: Once the three adjuncts are exhausted, they will again become Jincheng's disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Bankrupt the three adjuncts and you recreate Jincheng's disaster at home.

sentences.zh: 參前數言宜□西域，乃為西州士大夫所笑。

translations.literal: Shen earlier several times said they should abandon the Western Regions (one character missing in received text); western gentlemen of the capital laughed at him.
translations.idiomatic: I have urged abandoning the Western Regions before and was mocked by western scholars at court.

sentences.zh: 今苟貪不毛之地，營恤不使之民，[三]暴軍伊吾之野，以慮三族之外，[四]果破涼州，禍亂至今。

translations.literal: Now if they greedily cling to barren land, fret over peoples they cannot employ, [three] expose the army on the plain of Yiwu to worry beyond the three clans, [four] it truly broke Liangzhou and the calamity continues to today.
translations.idiomatic: Clinging to useless desert, coddling tribes we cannot use, and camping armies at Yiwu has already shattered Liangzhou and the trouble persists.

sentences.zh: 夫拓境不寧，無益於強；

translations.literal: To widen borders without peace brings no benefit to strength;
translations.idiomatic: Conquest without stability buys no real power.

sentences.zh: 多田不耕，何救饑敝！

translations.literal: Many fields untilled—how does that relieve hunger and exhaustion!
translations.idiomatic: More land left fallow cannot feed anyone.

sentences.zh: 故善為國者，務懷其內，不求外利；

translations.literal: Therefore those skilled at governing the state work to cherish what is within, not seek profit without;
translations.idiomatic: Wise rulers secure the interior before chasing outer gains.

sentences.zh: 務富其民，不貪廣土。

translations.literal: They work to enrich their people, not covet vast territory.
translations.idiomatic: They enrich the people instead of hoarding empty acres.

sentences.zh: 三輔山原曠遠，民庶稀疏，故縣丘城，可居者多。

translations.literal: The hills and plains of the three adjuncts are broad and far, population sparse; old county seats and ruined walls—many may be dwelt in.
translations.idiomatic: The capital districts hold vast uplands, thinly peopled, with abandoned towns ready for resettlement.

sentences.zh: [五]今宜徙邊郡不能自存者，入居諸陵，田戍故縣。

translations.literal: [Five] Now it is fitting to move those border commanderies unable to survive on their own to dwell among the imperial tombs, farm and garrison the old counties.
translations.idiomatic: Relocate vulnerable border counties into the tomb districts and put them to farming the old cantonments.

sentences.zh: 孤城絕郡，以權徙之；

translations.literal: Isolated cities and cut-off commanderies—shift them by expedient;
translations.idiomatic: Abandon hopeless garrisons and pull the people back.

sentences.zh: 轉運遠費，聚而近之；

translations.literal: distant transport costs—gather them near;
translations.idiomatic: Stop shipping supplies across half the empire.

sentences.zh: 徭役煩數，休而息之。

translations.literal: Corvée vexing and repeated—rest and let them breathe.
translations.idiomatic: Cut the endless labor drafts.

sentences.zh: 此善之善者也。

translations.literal: This is the best of good policies.
translations.idiomatic: That is the best policy of all.

sentences.zh: 」騭及公卿以國用不足，欲從參議，觿多不同，乃止。

translations.literal: " Zhi and the excellencies because state funds were insufficient wished to follow Shen's plan, but the many did not agree, and it stopped.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Zhi and the ministers liked the plan but lacked funds and factional support, so nothing came of it.

sentences.zh: 注[一]責音側懈反。

translations.literal: Comment one: Ze is read with the ce-xie fanqie spelling.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: The word for debt is glossed ce-xie.

sentences.zh: 注[二]為羌寇所傷也。

translations.literal: Comment two: Wounded by Qiang raids.
translations.idiomatic: Note 2: Meaning losses from Qiang attacks.

sentences.zh: 注[三]恤，憂也。

translations.literal: Comment three: Xu means worry.
translations.idiomatic: Note 3: Xu means to fret over.

sentences.zh: 不使之人謂戎虜凶獷，不堪為用。

translations.literal: "Peoples one cannot employ" means savage Rong and Di, too fierce to be used.
translations.idiomatic: The phrase means barbarians too wild to employ loyally.

sentences.zh: 注[四]言勞師救遠，以為親戚之憂慮。

translations.literal: Comment four: It speaks of tiring the army to save the distant, making it a care for kinsmen beyond the three clans.
translations.idiomatic: Note 4: Far campaigns exhaust the army and frighten families at home.

sentences.zh: 注[五]丘，空也。

translations.literal: Comment five: Qiu means empty.
translations.idiomatic: Note 5: Qiu here means ruined or empty.

sentences.zh: 拜參為漢陽太守。

translations.literal: Shen was appointed grand warden of Hanyang.
translations.idiomatic: The court named him grand warden of Hanyang.

sentences.zh: 郡人任棠者，有奇節，隱居教授。

translations.literal: There was a commandery man Ren Tang who had singular integrity, lived in reclusion and taught.
translations.idiomatic: A recluse named Ren Tang taught students in seclusion.

sentences.zh: 參到，先候之。

translations.literal: When Shen arrived, he first paid him a visit.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Shen called on him first.

sentences.zh: 棠不與言，但以薤一大本，水一盂，置戶屏前，自抱孫兒伏於戶下。

translations.literal: Tang did not speak with him but only placed a large bunch of allium, one bowl of water, before the door screen, himself hugging a grandson and crouching below the door.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Tang said nothing: he set a bowl of water and a huge scallion on his doorstep and crouched beneath the lintel hugging a child.

sentences.zh: 主簿白以為倨。

translations.literal: The chief clerk reported it as arrogance.
translations.idiomatic: His chief clerk thought it insulting.

sentences.zh: 參思其微意，良久曰：「棠是欲曉太守也。

translations.literal: Shen pondered the subtle meaning, long said: "Tang wishes to instruct the grand warden.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Shen reflected, then said, "He is teaching the new governor.

sentences.zh: 水者，欲吾清也。

translations.literal: Water means he wants me to be clear.
translations.idiomatic: The water means he wants purity.

sentences.zh: 拔大本薤者，欲吾擊強宗也。

translations.literal: Pulling up a large-rooted allium means he wants me to strike powerful lineages.
translations.idiomatic: The scallion with thick roots means strike down powerful clans.

sentences.zh: 抱兒當戶，欲吾開門恤孤也。

translations.literal: Hugging a child blocking the door means he wants me to open the gate and pity orphans.
translations.idiomatic: The child at the door means open your heart to widows and orphans.

sentences.zh: 」於是歎息而還。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he sighed and returned.
translations.idiomatic: He bowed and withdrew in admiration.

sentences.zh: 參在職，果能抑強助弱，以惠政得民。

translations.literal: Shen in office truly was able to suppress the strong and help the weak, and by kind government won the people.
translations.idiomatic: As magistrate he humbled magnates, helped the weak, and ruled with mercy.

sentences.zh: 元初元年，遷護羌校尉，畔羌懷其恩信。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yuanchu he was transferred protector of the Qiang; rebel Qiang cherished his grace and trust.
translations.idiomatic: In 114 CE he became protector of the Qiang; the tribes trusted him.

sentences.zh: 明年，燒當羌種號多等皆降，始復得還都令居，通河西路。

translations.literal: The next year the Shaodang Qiang chieftain Haoduo and others all surrendered; they first again were able to return and garrison Lingju, opening the Hexi road.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Haoduo of the Shaodang surrendered, allowing Han garrisons to return to Lingju and reopen the Hexi corridor.

sentences.zh: [一]時先零羌豪僭號北地，詔參將降羌及湟中義從胡七千人，[二]與行征西將軍司馬鈞期會北地擊之。

translations.literal: [One] At the time a Xianling Qiang chief falsely styled himself emperor in Beidi; an edict ordered Shen to lead surrendered Qiang and seven thousand volunteer Hu from Huangzhong, [two] to join acting general on the west Sima Jun at Beidi to strike them.
translations.idiomatic: When a Xianling chief declared himself in Beidi, Shen was ordered to lead seven thousand allied Qiang and Hu to rendezvous with Sima Jun.

sentences.zh: 參於道為羌所敗。

translations.literal: Shen on the road was defeated by Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Shen was ambushed en route and beaten.

sentences.zh: 既已失期，乃稱病引兵還，坐以詐疾征下獄，校書郎中馬融上書請之曰：「伏見西戎反畔，寇鈔五州，陛下愍百姓之傷痍，哀黎元之失業，單竭府庫以奉軍師。

translations.literal: Having already missed the rendezvous, he claimed illness and led the army back; for feigning illness he was summoned down to prison; collator gentleman Ma Rong submitted a memorial pleading: "Your subject has seen the western Rong rebel, raiding five provinces; Your Majesty pities the common people's wounds, grieves the black-haired people's loss of livelihood, exhausts the treasuries to supply the army.
translations.idiomatic: Missing his link-up, he feigned illness and retreated; the court jailed him for malingering. Ma Rong argued: "The west is ablaze across five provinces; you have emptied the treasury to fight it.

sentences.zh: 昔周宣獫狁侵鎬及方，[三]孝文匈奴亦略上郡，而宣王立中興之功，文帝建太宗之號。

translations.literal: Formerly King Xuan's Xianyun invaded Hao and Fang; [three] Emperor Wen's Xiongnu also raided Shang commandery, yet Xuan king achieved restoration merit, Wen established the Grand Founder title.
translations.idiomatic: King Xuan and Emperor Wen both faced barbarian raids yet became models of recovery.

sentences.zh: 非惟兩主有明叡之姿，抑亦扞城有虓虎之助，[四]是以南仲赫赫，列在周詩，亞夫赳赳，載於漢策。

translations.literal: Not only did the two lords have bright sagely bearing, but also their shield-walls had tiger-fierce aid; [four] therefore Nanzhong shone in the Zhou odes, Yafu stalwart is recorded in Han annals.
translations.idiomatic: They succeeded because they kept fierce generals at their side—Nanzhong in the Odes, Zhou Yafu in the histories.

sentences.zh: [五]竊見前護羌校尉龐參，文武昭備，智略弘遠，既有義勇果毅之節，兼以博雅深謀之姿。

translations.literal: [Five] Your subject has seen former protector of the Qiang Pang Shen: civil and martial fully prepared, counsel broad and far, having both righteous courage and resolute firmness, together with erudite depth of planning.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Shen combines courage and learning fit for command.

sentences.zh: 又度遼將軍梁慬，前統西域，勤苦數年，還留三輔，功□克立，閒在北邊，單于降服。

translations.literal: Further General Who Crosses the Liao Liang Jin, earlier commanded the Western Regions, toiled bitterly several years, returned to stay in the three adjuncts, merit (lacuna) was able to be established, lately on the northern border, the Shanyu submitted.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Jin pacified the west, then guarded the north until the Shanyu yielded.

sentences.zh: 今皆幽囚，陷於法網。

translations.literal: Now both are imprisoned, trapped in the law net.
translations.idiomatic: Now both generals rot in jail.

sentences.zh: 昔荀林父敗績於邲，晉侯使復其位；

translations.literal: Formerly Xun Linfu was routed at Bi; the marquis of Jin ordered him restored;
translations.idiomatic: Jin forgave Xun Linfu after his defeat at Bi.

sentences.zh: [六]孟明視喪師於崤，秦伯不替其官。

translations.literal: [six] Meng Mingshi lost the army at Yao; the earl of Qin did not remove him from office.
translations.idiomatic: Qin kept Meng Mingshi after his disaster at Yao.

sentences.zh: [七] 故晉景並赤狄之土，秦穆遂霸西戎。

translations.literal: [seven] Therefore Jin Jing annexed the Red Di lands, Qin Mu then hegemonized the western Rong.
translations.idiomatic: Forgiveness let Jin Jing conquer the Red Di and Qin Mu master the western tribes.

sentences.zh: [八]宜遠覽二君，使參、慬得在寬宥之科，誠有益於折衝，毗佐於聖化。

translations.literal: [eight] It is fitting to take distant view of those two lords, let Shen and Jin fall within the category of lenient pardon; truly there will be benefit for repelling assault and assisting sagely transformation.
translations.idiomatic: Do as those rulers did: pardon Shen and Jin and you strengthen the realm.

sentences.zh: 」書奏，赦參等。

translations.literal: " When the memorial was submitted, Shen and the others were pardoned.
translations.idiomatic: The throne pardoned Pang Shen and his fellows.

sentences.zh: 注[一]令居，縣，屬金城郡。

translations.literal: Comment one: Lingju, a county, belonged to Jincheng commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: Lingju County lay in Jincheng commandery.

sentences.zh: 令音零。

translations.literal: Ling is read like ling.
translations.idiomatic: The county name Lingju uses the ling reading.

sentences.zh: 注[二]湟，水名，今在鄯州。

translations.literal: Comment two: Huang is a river name, today in Shanzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Note 2: The Huang River is in modern Haidong (Shanzhou).

sentences.zh: 注[三]詩小雅六月之詩曰：「侵鎬及方，至於涇陽。

translations.literal: Comment three: The "Minor Odes" poem "Sixth Month" says: "They invaded Hao and Fang, reaching Jingyang.
translations.idiomatic: Note 3: The Odes describe Xianyun reaching Jingyang.

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注云：「鎬、方皆北方地名。」

translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan's commentary says: "Hao and Fang are both northern place names.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan identifies them as northern toponyms.

sentences.zh: 注[四]詩曰：「公侯干城。

translations.literal: Comment four: The Odes say: "The lord is shield-wall and tower.
translations.idiomatic: Note 4: The Odes praise lords as bulwarks.

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「闞如虓虎。

translations.literal: " Again it says: "Fierce as a roaring tiger.
translations.idiomatic: Another line compares warriors to tigers.

sentences.zh: 」干，扞也。

translations.literal: " Gan means shield.
translations.idiomatic: Gan means to shield.

sentences.zh: 虓虎，怒貌也。

translations.literal: Tiger-roar means an angry appearance.
translations.idiomatic: The image is of raging tigers.

sentences.zh: 注[五]詩曰：「赫赫南仲，薄伐西戎。

translations.literal: Comment five: The Odes say: "Bright was Nanzhong, punishing the western Rong.
translations.idiomatic: Note 5: Nanzhong's western campaign appears in the Odes.

sentences.zh: 」周亞夫為漢將。

translations.literal: " Zhou Yafu was a Han general.
translations.idiomatic: The line recalls Zhou Yafu, the stern Han commander who saved Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 赳赳，武貌。

translations.literal: Stalwart means a martial bearing.
translations.idiomatic: The reduplicative describes martial bearing.

sentences.zh: 注[六]左傳曰，晉荀林父及楚師戰於邲，晉師敗績。

translations.literal: Comment six: The Zuo says: Jin Xun Linfu met Chu army at Bi; Jin army was routed.
translations.idiomatic: Note 6: The Zuo Tradition narrates the rout at Bi.

sentences.zh: 林父請死，晉侯欲許之。

translations.literal: Linfu asked death; the marquis of Jin wished to grant it.
translations.idiomatic: Xun asked to die; the duke first agreed.

sentences.zh: 士貞子諫曰：「不可。

translations.literal: Shi Zhenzi remonstrated saying: "It cannot be.
translations.idiomatic: Shi Zhenzi dissuaded him.

sentences.zh: 夫其敗也，如日月之食，何損於明？

translations.literal: As for defeat, it is like eclipse of sun and moon—what harm to their brightness?
translations.idiomatic: Defeat is like an eclipse: it does not dim the heavens for long.

sentences.zh: 」晉侯使復其位。

translations.literal: " The marquis of Jin ordered him restored to his post.
translations.idiomatic: The duke reinstated Xun Linfu.

sentences.zh: 注[七]左傳曰，晉敗秦師於崤，獲百里孟明視，後赦而歸之。

translations.literal: Comment seven: The Zuo says: Jin defeated Qin army at Yao, captured Baili Meng Mingshi, later pardoned and returned him.
translations.idiomatic: Note 7: Jin captured Meng Mingshi at Yao but later released him.

sentences.zh: 秦伯曰：「孤之罪也。

translations.literal: The earl of Qin said: "It is orphan's crime.
translations.idiomatic: The earl of Qin blamed himself.

sentences.zh: 」不替孟明。

translations.literal: " He did not remove Meng Ming.
translations.idiomatic: He kept Meng Mingshi in command.

sentences.zh: 注[八]左傳曰，晉荀林父敗赤狄，遂滅之。

translations.literal: Comment eight: The Zuo says: Jin Xun Linfu defeated the Red Di and thereupon extinguished them.
translations.idiomatic: Note 8: Xun Linfu destroyed the Red Di.

sentences.zh: 晉侯賞林父狄臣千室，亦賞士貞子瓜衍之縣，曰：「吾獲狄土，子之功也。

translations.literal: The marquis of Jin rewarded Linfu with a thousand Di households; also rewarded Shi Zhenzi with Guayan county, saying: "We gained Di lands; it is your merit.
translations.idiomatic: The duke enfeoffed both generals for the Di campaign.

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「秦伯伐晉，遂霸西戎，用孟明也。」

translations.literal: " Again it says: "The earl of Qin attacked Jin, thereupon hegemonized the western Rong, employing Meng Ming.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo adds that Qin later used Meng Ming to dominate the west.

sentences.zh: 後以參為遼東太守。

translations.literal: Later Shen was made grand warden of Liaodong.
translations.idiomatic: He was later posted grand warden of Liaodong.

sentences.zh: 永建元年，遷度遼將軍。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongjian he was transferred General Who Crosses the Liao.
translations.idiomatic: In 126 CE he became General Who Crosses the Liao.

sentences.zh: 四年，入為大鴻臚。

translations.literal: In the fourth year he entered as grand herald.
translations.idiomatic: In 129 CE he entered court as grand herald.

sentences.zh: 尚書僕射虞詡薦參有宰相器能，*

translations.literal: Vice director of the secretariat Yu Xu recommended Shen as having prime minister capacity, *
translations.idiomatic: Yu Xu of the secretariat urged that Pang Shen had the makings of a chief minister (lacuna follows).

sentences.zh: (順帝時)

translations.literal: (during Emperor Shun)
translations.idiomatic: The lacuna marks Emperor Shun's reign.

sentences.zh: *以為太尉，錄尚書事。

translations.literal: *and appointed him grand commandant, recorder of masters of writing.
translations.idiomatic: The court then made him grand commandant with authority over the secretariat.

sentences.zh: 是時三公之中，參名忠直，數為左右所陷毀，以所舉用忤帝旨，司隸承風案之。

translations.literal: At that time among the three excellencies Shen's name was loyal and upright; repeatedly he was slandered by those at court; because his recommendations offended the emperor's intent, the colonel director of retainers followed the wind and investigated him.
translations.idiomatic: Though famed for honesty among the three dukes, he was slandered at court; his nominations irked the emperor and the colonel director of retainers hounded him.

sentences.zh: 時當會茂才孝廉，參以被奏，稱疾不得會。

translations.literal: At the time they were to convene abundant talent and filial incorrupt; Shen because he had been memorialized claimed illness and could not attend.
translations.idiomatic: When the court summoned candidates for office, he stayed away pleading illness after impeachment.

sentences.zh: 上計掾廣漢段恭因會上疏曰：「伏見道路行人，農夫織婦，皆曰『太尉龐參，竭忠盡節，徒以直道不能曲心，孤立腢邪之閒，自處中傷之地』。

translations.literal: Chief clerk of accounts Duan Gong of Guanghan therefore at the meeting submitted a memorial saying: "Your subject has seen travelers on the road, farmers and weaving wives, all saying Grand Commandant Pang Shen exhausts loyalty and utmost integrity; only because the straight way cannot bend the heart, he stands alone between crafty evil, placing himself where he is wounded.
translations.idiomatic: His accounts clerk Duan Gong reported that every farmer on the road praised Pang Shen's loyalty yet pitied him for refusing to flatter the powerful.

sentences.zh: 臣猶冀在陛下之世，當蒙安全，而復以讒佞傷毀忠正，此天地之大禁，人主之至誡。

translations.literal: Your subject still hoped in Your Majesty's age he would receive safety; yet again slanderers wound and destroy the loyal and upright—this is heaven and earth's great prohibition, the ruler's utmost warning.
translations.idiomatic: To let calumny destroy such a man breaks heaven's rule and a sovereign's first duty.

sentences.zh: 昔白起賜死，諸侯酌酒相賀；

translations.literal: Formerly Bai Qi was ordered to die and the feudal lords poured wine to congratulate one another;
translations.idiomatic: When Qin forced Bai Qi to suicide, rival states toasted the news.

sentences.zh: 季子來歸，魯人喜其紓難。

translations.literal: When Jizi returned, the people of Lu rejoiced that their troubles were eased.
translations.idiomatic: When Prince Jiyou returned to Lu, the people rejoiced at deliverance.

sentences.zh: [一]夫國以賢化，君以忠安。

translations.literal: [One] For the state is transformed by worthies, the lord is secured by loyalty.
translations.idiomatic: States thrive on worthy ministers and loyal service.

sentences.zh: 今天下鹹欣陛下有此忠賢，願卒寵任，以安社稷。

translations.literal: Now the realm all rejoices that Your Majesty has this loyal worthy; may you finally favor and employ him to secure the altars of soil and grain.
translations.idiomatic: All under heaven delights that you have such a man; keep him at the helm to steady the state.

sentences.zh: 」書奏，詔即遣小黃門視參疾，太醫致羊酒。

translations.literal: " When the memorial was submitted, an edict at once sent a junior yellow gate to view Shen's illness, the imperial physician sent mutton and wine.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent a eunuch physician with food and wine to inquire after him.

sentences.zh: 注[一]紓，緩也。

translations.literal: Comment one: Shu means to ease.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: Shu means relief from distress.

sentences.zh: 季子，魯公子季友也。

translations.literal: Jizi is Lu prince Jiyou.
translations.idiomatic: Jizi is Prince Jiyou of Lu.

sentences.zh: 閔公之時，國家多難，以季子忠賢，故請齊侯復之。

translations.literal: In Duke Min's time the state had many troubles; because Jizi was loyal and worthy, therefore they asked the marquis of Qi to restore him.
translations.idiomatic: During Duke Min's troubles Lu begged Qi to restore the loyal prince.

sentences.zh: 公羊傳曰：「季子來歸。

translations.literal: The Gongyang says: "Jizi came home.
translations.idiomatic: The Gongyang commentary records his return.

sentences.zh: 其言季子何？

translations.literal: Why does it say Jizi?
translations.idiomatic: Why is he called Jizi?

sentences.zh: 賢也。

translations.literal: Because he was worthy.
translations.idiomatic: The commentary answers: to honor his virtue.

sentences.zh: 言其來歸何？

translations.literal: Why does it speak of his coming home?
translations.idiomatic: Why stress his return?

sentences.zh: 喜之也。」

translations.literal: It means people rejoiced in it.
translations.idiomatic: The Gongyang gloss ends: the people rejoiced at his return.

sentences.zh: 後參夫人疾前妻子，投於井而殺之。

translations.literal: Later Shen's wife, ill toward her stepson by a former wife, threw him in a well and killed him.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Shen's wife murdered her stepson by casting him down a well.

sentences.zh: 參素與洛陽令祝良不平，[一]良聞之，率吏卒入太尉府案實其事，乃上參罪，遂因災異策免。

translations.literal: Shen by nature was on bad terms with Luoyang magistrate Zhu Liang; [one] Liang heard of it, led clerks and soldiers into the grand commandant's office to investigate the facts, then memorialized Shen's crime; thereupon because of portents he was dismissed by edict.
translations.idiomatic: His enemy Zhu Liang raided the grand commandant's yamen, proved the murder, impeached Pang Shen, and had him cashiered over ill omens.

sentences.zh: 有司以良不先聞奏，輒折辱宰相，坐系詔獄。

translations.literal: The responsible officials because Liang had not first reported upward but rashly humiliated the chief minister sentenced him bound in the imperial prison.
translations.idiomatic: The ministry jailed Zhu Liang for confronting a chief minister without prior imperial leave.

sentences.zh: 良能得百姓心，洛陽吏人守闕請代其罪者，日有數千萬人，詔乃原刑。

translations.literal: Liang was able to win the common people's hearts; Luoyang clerks and commoners guarding the palace gate asking to substitute for his crime were daily several thousand myriad persons; an edict then pardoned the sentence.
translations.idiomatic: Thousands thronged the palace gate offering to serve Zhu Liang's sentence until the emperor pardoned him.

sentences.zh: 注[一]謝承書曰「良字邵平，長沙人。

translations.literal: Comment one: Xie Cheng's book says: "Liang styled Shaoping, a man of Changsha.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: Xie Cheng describes Zhu Liang as a talented native of Changsha.

sentences.zh: 聰明博學有才幹，以廉平見稱」也。

translations.literal: Clever and broad in learning with ability, by incorrupt fairness he was praised.
translations.idiomatic: He was praised as clever, learned, and fair.

sentences.zh: 陽嘉四年，復以參為太尉。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yangjia Shen was again made grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In 135 CE Pang Shen was restored as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 永和元年，以久病罷，卒於家。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yonghe he was dismissed for long illness and died at home.
translations.idiomatic: In 136 CE he retired ill and died at home.

sentences.zh: 陳龜字叔珍，上黨泫氏人也。

translations.literal: Chen Gui, courtesy name Shuzhen, was a man of Xuanshi in Shangdang.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Gui, courtesy name Shuzhen, came from Xuanshi in Shangdang.

sentences.zh: [一]家世邊將，便習弓馬，雄於北州。

translations.literal: [One] For generations his family were border generals; he was practiced in bow and horse and heroic in the northern province.
translations.idiomatic: His clan had long guarded the frontier; he excelled at arms and was famed in the north.

sentences.zh: 注[一]泫氏故城，今澤州高平縣也。

translations.literal: Comment one: The old city of Xuanshi is today Gaoping County in Zezhou.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: Xuanshi lay at present-day Gaoping in Shanxi.

sentences.zh: 泫音公玄反。

translations.literal: Xuan is read with the gong-xuan fanqie spelling.
translations.idiomatic: The place name is glossed gong-xuan.

sentences.zh: 龜少有志氣。

translations.literal: Gui in youth had ambition and spirit.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Gui was ambitious from boyhood.

sentences.zh: 永建中，舉孝廉，五遷五原太守。

translations.literal: In the Yongjian era he was recommended filial and incorrupt, after five transfers became grand warden of Wuyuan.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as filial and incorrupt, he rose through five posts to grand warden of Wuyuan.

sentences.zh: 永和五年，拜使匈奴中郎將。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of Yonghe he was appointed chief commandant to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: In 140 CE he became chief commandant over the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 時南匈奴左部反亂，龜以單于不能制下，外順內畔，促令自殺，坐征下獄免。

translations.literal: At the time the southern Xiongnu left section rebelled; Gui because the Shanyu could not control those below, outwardly compliant but inwardly rebelled, urged him to commit suicide; he was summoned down to prison and dismissed after conviction.
translations.idiomatic: When the southern left wing revolted, Chen Gui blamed the Shanyu for losing control and forced him to suicide; the court jailed Chen Gui and stripped his office.

sentences.zh: 後再遷，拜京兆尹。

translations.literal: Later after two transfers he was appointed metropolitan governor.
translations.idiomatic: He later became metropolitan governor of the capital region.

sentences.zh: 時三輔強豪之族，多侵枉小民。

translations.literal: At the time powerful magnate clans of the three adjuncts often encroached on and wronged common people.
translations.idiomatic: Great families in the three capital districts oppressed the poor.

sentences.zh: 龜到，厲威嚴，悉平理其怨屈者，郡內大悅。

translations.literal: When Gui arrived, he enforced awe-inspiring dignity and fully settled all who had grievances; within the commandery there was great joy.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Gui restored justice to every petitioner and the capital district rejoiced.

sentences.zh: 會羌胡寇邊，殺長吏，驅略百姓。

translations.literal: It happened that Qiang and Hu raided the border, killed senior officials, and drove off commoners.
translations.idiomatic: Then Qiang and Hu raided the frontier, killing magistrates and driving off settlers.

sentences.zh: 桓帝以龜世諳邊俗，拜為度遼將軍。

translations.literal: Emperor Huan because Gui for generations was versed in border ways appointed him General Who Crosses the Liao.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan named him General Who Crosses the Liao for his family's frontier expertise.

sentences.zh: 龜臨行，上疏曰：「臣龜蒙恩累世，馳騁邊垂，雖展鷹犬之用，頓斃胡虜之庭，魂骸不返，薦享狐狸，猶無以塞厚責，荅萬分也。

translations.literal: Gui on the eve of departure submitted a memorial saying: "Your subject Gui has received favor for generations, galloping on the border marches; though he might display hound and hawk use, fall dead in the Hu bandits' court, soul and bones not return, offered to foxes, still it would not fill heavy blame or answer one myriadth.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Gui's farewell memorial vowed to die on the frontier yet feared he could never repay the throne's kindness.

translations.literal: Lacuna marker in received text.
translations.idiomatic: The manuscript shows damage here.

translations.literal: (to)
translations.idiomatic: The next fragment reads the preposition to.

sentences.zh: *臣*[至]*頑駑，器無鈆刀一割之用，過受國恩，榮秩兼優，生年死日，永懼不報。

translations.literal: Your subject is a dull nag of a man, a vessel with no lead knife's single cut of use, yet I have received excessive state grace, rank and stipend both ample; from the day I was born to the day I die I forever fear I cannot repay.
translations.idiomatic: I am no sharp blade yet I have been overpaid; I may never repay what I owe the throne.

sentences.zh: 臣聞三辰不軌，擢士為相；

translations.literal: Your subject has heard when the three luminaries stray from their tracks, promote a scholar to be minister;
translations.idiomatic: When heaven misaligns, the sage promotes wise ministers.

sentences.zh: 蠻夷不恭，拔卒為將。

translations.literal: When barbarians are not reverent, raise a soldier to be general.
translations.idiomatic: When tribes rebel, promote soldiers to generals.

sentences.zh: 臣無文武之才，而忝鷹揚之任，[一]上籩聖*

translations.literal: Your subject lacks civil and martial talent, yet disgraces the hawk-soaring office; [one] above he fouls the sagely *
translations.idiomatic: He admits he lacks skill yet holds a general's commission, shaming the court above.

translations.literal: (court)
translations.idiomatic: Lacuna supplies the word court.

sentences.zh: **[明]*，下懼素餐，[二]雖歿軀體，無所云補。

translations.literal: **bright** below he fears empty eating; [two] though body perishes, there is nothing to say of aid.
translations.idiomatic: Below he fears eating salary without merit and dying useless to the state.

sentences.zh: 今西州邊鄙，土地塉埆，[三]鞍馬為居，射獵為業，男寡耕稼之利，女乏機杼之饒，守塞候望，懸命鋒鏑，聞急長驅，去不圖反。

translations.literal: Now the western province's border is remote, land stony and thin; [three] saddles and horses are dwelling, hunting and shooting are livelihood; men lack plowing and harvest profit, women lack loom and shuttle plenty; guarding passes and watching beacons, life hangs on blade and arrow; hearing alarm they gallop far, leaving without planning return.
translations.idiomatic: The northwest is barren: people live in the saddle, men cannot farm, women cannot weave, and every scout stakes life on the frontier.

sentences.zh: 自頃年以來，匈奴數攻營郡，[四]殘殺長吏，侮略良細。

translations.literal: In recent years the Xiongnu repeatedly attacked garrison commanderies; [four] slaughtered senior officials, insulted and plundered good commoners.
translations.idiomatic: For years the Xiongnu have stormed garrison towns and butchered officials and civilians.

sentences.zh: 戰夫身膏沙漠，居人首繫馬鞍。

translations.literal: Fighters smear the desert with their bodies, residents hang heads on saddlebows.
translations.idiomatic: Warriors rot in the sand while civilians' heads decorate saddlebows.

sentences.zh: 或舉國掩戶，盡種灰滅，孤兒寡婦，號哭空城，野無青草，室如懸磬。

translations.literal: Sometimes whole states shut doors, entire seed turned to ash and extinction, orphans and widows wail in empty cities, wilds without green grass, houses like suspended stone chimes.
translations.idiomatic: Whole districts are wiped out; orphans wail in ruined towns.

sentences.zh: [五]雖含生氣，實同枯朽。

translations.literal: [Five] Though they contain living breath, in truth they are the same as withered wood.
translations.idiomatic: The living there are as good as dead wood.

sentences.zh: 往歲并州水雨，災螟互生，稼穡荒耗，租更空闕。

translations.literal: In past years Bingzhou had floods and rain, disaster and locusts arose together, crops wasted away, rent and rotation duty stood empty.
translations.idiomatic: Bingzhou has suffered flood, locusts, and crop failure until taxes cannot be collected.

sentences.zh: [六]老者慮不終年，少壯懼於困□。

translations.literal: [Six] Old people worry they will not finish the year, young and strong fear distress in the months ahead (one character missing in received text).
translations.idiomatic: The elderly expect to die before winter; the young dread the lean months ahead.

sentences.zh: 陛下以百姓為子，品庶以陛下為父，焉可不日□勞神，[七]垂撫循之恩哉！

translations.literal: Your Majesty takes the hundred surnames as children, the multitude takes Your Majesty as father—how can you not daily labor your spirit, [seven] let fall the grace of soothing care!
translations.idiomatic: The people are your children; how can you refuse to weary yourself daily comforting them?

sentences.zh: 唐堯親捨其子以禪虞舜者，是欲民遭聖君，不令遇惡主也。

translations.literal: Tang Yao personally yielded his son to abdicate to Shun of Yu because he wished the people to meet a sage lord, not order them to meet a wicked lord.
translations.idiomatic: Yao passed the throne to Shun rather than to a worthless son so the people would have a sage ruler.

sentences.zh: [八]故古公杖策，其民五倍；

translations.literal: [Eight] Therefore the ancient duke leaned on his staff and his people multiplied fivefold;
translations.idiomatic: The ancient Duke of Bin led his people west and his following multiplied.

sentences.zh: [九] 文王西伯，天下歸之。

translations.literal: [Nine] King Wen as western earl, the realm returned to him.
translations.idiomatic: King Wen as lord of the west drew all hearts to Zhou.

sentences.zh: [一０]豈復輿金輦寶，以為民惠乎！

translations.literal: [Ten] Did he again haul gold carriages and treasure to curry favor with the people!
translations.idiomatic: He did not need bribes to win the people.

sentences.zh: 近孝文皇帝感一女子之言，除肉刑之法，[一一]體德行仁，為漢賢主。

translations.literal: Recently Filial Emperor Wen was moved by one woman's words, abolished the flesh mutilation laws; [eleven] embodying virtue and practicing benevolence, he became Han's worthy lord.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen abolished mutilating punishments after one girl's plea—such benevolence made him a model ruler.

sentences.zh: 陛下繼中興之統，承光武之業，臨朝聽政，而未留聖意。

translations.literal: Your Majesty continues restoration's line, receives Guangwu's enterprise, faces court and hears governance, yet has not fixed sage intent.
translations.idiomatic: You inherit the restored Han yet have not matched those examples.

sentences.zh: 且牧守不良，或出中官，懼逆上旨，取過目前。

translations.literal: Moreover shepherds and guardians are not good, some come from palace attendants, fearing to oppose superior intent, taking fault before the eyes.
translations.idiomatic: Many magistrates are eunuchs' creatures who dare not cross the palace.

sentences.zh: 呼嗟之聲，招致災害，胡虜凶悍，因衰緣隙。

translations.literal: Wailing sounds summon disasters; Hu and Lu are fierce, taking advantage of decline and gap.
translations.idiomatic: Popular groans summon omens; barbarians strike when the court weakens.

sentences.zh: 而令倉庫單于豺狼之口，功業無銖兩之□，皆由將帥不忠，聚奸所致。

translations.literal: Yet you let granaries fall into wolf and jackal mouths, merit without a scruple of weight (one character missing in received text), all because generals are not loyal, gathering wickedness brings it about.
translations.idiomatic: Treasury grain feeds wolves while generals register no gain—because commanders are corrupt.

sentences.zh: 前涼州刺史祝良，初除到州，多所糾罰，太守令長，貶黜將半，政未踰時，功□卓然。

translations.literal: Former Bingzhou governor Zhu Liang, on first appointment to the province, investigated and punished much; grand wardens and magistrates, demoted and dismissed nearly half; government had not passed a season, merit already stood out.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Liang once purged half the corrupt magistrates in Bingzhou in a single season.

sentences.zh: 實應賞異，以勸功能，改任牧守，去斥奸殘。

translations.literal: Truly he should receive unusual reward to encourage ability and work; change and appoint shepherds and guardians, cast off wicked remnants.
translations.idiomatic: Reward such men and replace bad governors.

sentences.zh: 又宜更選匈奴烏桓護羌中郎將校尉，簡練文武，授之法令，除並涼二州今年租更，寬赦罪隸，埽除更始。

translations.literal: Further it is fitting again to select chief commandants over the Xiongnu, Wuhuan, and Qiang, drill civil and martial men, grant them statutes and orders, remit this year's rent and rotation for Bing and Liang two provinces, broadly pardon convict laborers, sweep clean and begin anew.
translations.idiomatic: Reform the frontier commands, remit Bing and Liang taxes for a year, and free convict laborers for a fresh start.

sentences.zh: 則善吏知奉公之佑，惡者覺營私之禍，胡馬可不窺長城，塞下無候望之患矣。

translations.literal: Then good clerks will know the aid of serving the public, evil ones will feel the disaster of seeking private profit; Hu horses need not spy on the Long Wall, below the passes there will be no lookout calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Good officials will serve honestly, evil ones will fear exposure, and the steppe will not dare raid the Wall.

sentences.zh: 」帝覺悟，乃更選幽、井刺史，自營郡太守都尉以下，多所革易，下詔「為陳將軍除並、涼一年租賦，以賜吏民」。

translations.literal: " The emperor awoke, then again selected You and Bing governors; from garrison commandery grand warden and chief commandant downward many were changed; he issued an edict "for General Chen remitting one year's rent and tax for Bing and Liang to grant clerks and commoners.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan took the advice, replaced frontier officials, and remitted a year of taxes in Bing and Liang for Chen Gui's sake.

sentences.zh: 龜既到職，州郡重足震慄，鮮卑不敢近塞，省息經用，歲以億計。

translations.literal: When Gui had taken office, provinces and commanderies walked on tiptoe and trembled; the Xianbei did not dare approach the passes; he reduced routine expenses by yearly sums reckoned in the hundreds of millions.
translations.idiomatic: Under his command the Xianbei shunned the passes and he saved hundreds of millions yearly.

sentences.zh: [一二] 注[一]詩曰「維師尚父，時惟鷹揚」也。

translations.literal: [Twelve] Comment one: The Odes says "Grand Instructor Shangfu, then was the hawk soaring.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: The hawk-soaring line comes from the Book of Odes.

sentences.zh: 注[二]素，空也。

translations.literal: Comment two: Su means empty.
translations.idiomatic: Note 2: Su means empty-handed.

sentences.zh: 無功受祿為素餐。

translations.literal: Receiving stipend without merit is called empty eating.
translations.idiomatic: Eating salary without merit is the "empty meal" fault.

sentences.zh: 注[三]埆音覺，又音確，謂薄土也。

translations.literal: Comment three: Que is read jue, also que, meaning thin soil.
translations.idiomatic: Note 3: Que describes stony thin soil on the frontier.

sentences.zh: 注[四]謂郡有屯兵者，即護羌校尉屯金城，烏桓校尉屯上谷之類。

translations.literal: Comment four: It means commanderies that have garrison troops, such as the protector of the Qiang stationed at Jincheng, the Wuhuan colonel stationed at Shanggu, and the like.
translations.idiomatic: Note 4: Garrison commanderies include the Qiang protector at Jincheng and the Wuhuan colonel at Shanggu.

sentences.zh: 注[五]左傳曰：「室如懸磬，野無青草。

translations.literal: Comment five: The Zuo says: "Houses like suspended stone chimes, wilds without green grass.
translations.idiomatic: Note 5: The Zuo uses the empty larder image for famine.

sentences.zh: 」言其屋居如磬之懸，下無所有。

translations.literal: " It means dwelling like a suspended chime, below nothing possessed.
translations.idiomatic: It describes homes stripped bare.

sentences.zh: 注[六]更謂卒更錢也。

translations.literal: Comment six: Geng means the conscript rotation fee.
translations.idiomatic: Note 6: Geng here means the cash levy for corvée rotation.

sentences.zh: 注[七]書曰「文王至於日中□，不遑暇食」也。

translations.literal: Comment seven: The Documents says "King Wen until midday (lacuna) had no leisure to eat.
translations.idiomatic: Note 7: The Shang shu praises King Wen's tireless care for the people.

sentences.zh: 注[八]史記曰「堯知子丹朱不肖，不足授天下，乃推授舜。

translations.literal: Comment eight: The Records says "Yao knew his son Dan Zhu was unworthy, insufficient to receive the realm, and pushed the grant to Shun.
translations.idiomatic: Note 8: The Shi ji tells how Yao passed the throne to Shun.

sentences.zh: *[授舜]*則天下得其利而丹朱病，授丹朱則天下病而丹朱得其利。

translations.literal: Grant Shun then the realm gained its benefit and Dan Zhu fell ill; grant Dan Zhu then the realm fell ill and Dan Zhu gained its benefit.
translations.idiomatic: Giving the realm to Shun helped all; giving it to Dan Zhu would have harmed all.

sentences.zh: 堯曰：『終不以天下之病而利一人。

translations.literal: Yao said: "In the end I will not use the realm's illness to profit one man.
translations.idiomatic: Yao refused to enrich one son at the empire's cost.

sentences.zh: 』卒授舜以天下」也。

translations.literal: " In the end he granted Shun the realm.
translations.idiomatic: He gave the throne to Shun.

sentences.zh: 注[九]帝王世紀曰「古公但甫，是為太王，為百姓所附。

translations.literal: Comment nine: The August Diarchs Annals says "Ancient Duke Danfu, called Grand King, was attached to by the hundred surnames.
translations.idiomatic: Note 9: The ancient Duke of Bin fled the Rong and won a nation.

sentences.zh: 狄人攻之，事之以皮幣玉帛，不能免焉。

translations.literal: Di attacked him; he served them with hides, silk, and jade but could not escape.
translations.idiomatic: He tried to buy off the Di with tribute in vain.

sentences.zh: 王遂杖策而去，踰梁山，止於岐山之陽，邑於周地。

translations.literal: The king then leaned on his staff and left, crossed Liang Mountain, stopped south of Mount Qi, built a town on Zhou land.
translations.idiomatic: He led his people over Liangshan to the foot of Qi.

sentences.zh: 豳人從者如歸巿，一年成邑，二年成都，三年五倍其初」也。

translations.literal: Bin people who followed were like returning to market; in one year it became a town, in two years a city, in three years five times the beginning.
translations.idiomatic: His followers doubled yearly until his settlement flourished fivefold.

sentences.zh: 注[一０]帝王世紀曰西伯至仁，百姓襁負而至。

translations.literal: Comment ten: The August Diarchs Annals says the western earl was utmost benevolent; the hundred surnames carried babies on their backs to join him.
translations.idiomatic: Note 10: King Wen drew the people like a magnet.

sentences.zh: 注[一一]女子即太倉令淳于公之女緹縈也。

translations.literal: Comment eleven: The woman is the daughter of Grand Granary Director Chunyu, Ti Ying.
translations.idiomatic: Note 11: The girl who moved Emperor Wen was Ti Ying, daughter of the granary director.

sentences.zh: 事見前書。

translations.literal: The affair is seen in the Former Documents.
translations.idiomatic: Her story appears in the Han shu.

sentences.zh: 注[一二]經，常也。

translations.literal: Comment twelve: Jing means constant.
translations.idiomatic: Note 12: Jing means routine expenditure.

sentences.zh: 大將軍梁冀與龜素有隙，譖其沮毀國威，挑取功譽，[一]不為胡虜所畏。

translations.literal: Grand General Liang Ji with Gui by nature had a rift; he slandered him as damping state prestige and fishing for merit and praise, [one] not feared by Hu bandits.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Ji hated Chen Gui and accused him of cowardice and self-promotion.

sentences.zh: 坐征還，遂乞骸骨歸田裡。

translations.literal: He was summoned back and thereupon begged bones to return to village fields.
translations.idiomatic: Recalled in disgrace, Chen Gui retired to his estate.

sentences.zh: 復征為尚書。

translations.literal: Again he was summoned as minister of the secretariat.
translations.idiomatic: The court recalled him as minister of the secretariat.

sentences.zh: 冀暴虐日甚，龜上疏言其罪狀，請誅之。

translations.literal: Ji's violence grew daily worse; Gui submitted a memorial stating his crimes and requesting execution.
translations.idiomatic: He then memorialized Liang Ji's crimes and demanded his execution.

sentences.zh: 帝不省。

translations.literal: The emperor did not examine.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ignored him.

sentences.zh: 自知必為冀所害，不食七日而死。

translations.literal: Knowing he would surely be harmed by Ji, he did not eat seven days and died.
translations.idiomatic: Certain Liang Ji would murder him, he fasted seven days and died.

sentences.zh: 西域胡夷，並、涼民庶，鹹為舉哀，吊祭其墓。

translations.literal: Western Regions Hu and Yi, Bing and Liang common people, all for him raised mourning and sacrificed at his tomb.
translations.idiomatic: Frontier Hu and Han alike mourned him at his grave.

sentences.zh: 注[一]挑取猶獨取也。

translations.literal: Comment one: Tiao qu means alone taking.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: Tiao qu means to seize credit alone.

sentences.zh: 獨取其名，如挑戰之義。

translations.literal: Alone seizing the name, like the sense of issuing challenge.
translations.idiomatic: It implies picking a fight for glory.

sentences.zh: 橋玄字公祖，梁國睢陽人也。

translations.literal: Qiao Xuan, courtesy name Gongzu, was a man of Suiyang in Liang state.
translations.idiomatic: Qiao Xuan, courtesy name Gongzu, came from Suiyang in Liang.

sentences.zh: 七世祖仁，從同郡戴德學，著禮記章句四十九篇，號曰「橋君學」。

translations.literal: His seventh-generation ancestor Ren studied with Dai De of the same commandery, composed forty-nine sections of ritual glosses, called "Lord Qiao learning.
translations.idiomatic: His ancestor Qiao Ren wrote forty-nine chapters of ritual commentary called the Qiao school.

sentences.zh: 成帝時為大鴻臚。

translations.literal: In Emperor Cheng's time he was grand herald.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Cheng he served as grand herald.

sentences.zh: 祖父基，廣陵太守。

translations.literal: His grandfather Ji was grand warden of Guangling.
translations.idiomatic: His grandfather was grand warden of Guangling.

sentences.zh: 父肅，東萊太守。

translations.literal: His father Su was grand warden of Donglai.
translations.idiomatic: His father was grand warden of Donglai.

sentences.zh: 玄少為縣功曹。

translations.literal: Xuan in youth was commandery merit assessor.
translations.idiomatic: Qiao Xuan began as commandery merit assessor.

sentences.zh: 時豫州刺史周景行部到梁國，玄謁景，因伏地言陳相羊昌罪惡，乞為部陳從事，[一]窮案其奸。

translations.literal: At the time the inspector of Yu Province Zhou Jing conducted his circuit and reached Liang state; Xuan visited Jing, then prostrate on the ground stated Chancellor of Chen Yang Chang's crimes and evil, begging to be adjutant in charge of Chen, [one] to exhaustively investigate his wickedness.
translations.idiomatic: When Inspector Zhou Jing toured Liang, Qiao Xuan threw himself before the carriage denouncing Chancellor Yang Chang and volunteered as investigator.

sentences.zh: 景壯玄意，署而遣之。

translations.literal: Jing admired Xuan's intent, appointed him and sent him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Jing admired his nerve and gave him the commission.

sentences.zh: 玄到，悉收昌賓客，具考臧罪。

translations.literal: When Xuan arrived, he seized all of Chang's guests and fully examined bribery crimes.
translations.idiomatic: Qiao Xuan arrested Yang Chang's clients and proved his corruption.

sentences.zh: 昌素為大將軍梁冀所厚，冀為馳檄救之。

translations.literal: Chang by nature was thick with Grand General Liang Ji; Ji for him sent urgent dispatch to rescue.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Chang was Liang Ji's protégé; Liang Ji sent a rescue dispatch.

sentences.zh: 景承旨召玄，玄還檄不發，案之益急。

translations.literal: Jing received intent and summoned Xuan; Xuan returned dispatch without issuing, investigating more urgently.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Jing recalled him, but he refused to drop the case.

sentences.zh: 昌坐檻車征，玄由是著名。

translations.literal: Chang sat in caged cart summoned; Xuan from this became famous.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Chang went to the capital in a prison cart while Qiao Xuan's fame spread.

sentences.zh: 注[一]部猶領也。

translations.literal: Comment one: Bu means to lead.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: Bu here means to lead or head.

sentences.zh: 舉孝廉，補洛陽左尉。

translations.literal: Recommended filial and incorrupt, he supplemented Luoyang left captain.
translations.idiomatic: Nominated filial and incorrupt, he became Luoyang left captain.

sentences.zh: [一]時梁不疑為河南尹，玄以公事當詣府受對，恥為所辱，□官還鄉里。

translations.literal: [One] At the time Liang Buyi was governor of Henan; Xuan because of public business had to go to the office to receive questioning, shamed to be insulted by him, vacated his post and returned to home village.
translations.idiomatic: Rather than answer to Liang Buyi as governor of Henan on a petty charge, he resigned and went home.

sentences.zh: 後四遷為齊相，坐事為城旦。

translations.literal: Later after four transfers he became chancellor of Qi; for an affair he was sentenced wall-builder labor.
translations.idiomatic: Four promotions later he was chancellor of Qi until an offense reduced him to wall-builder convict labor.

sentences.zh: 刑竟，征，再遷上谷太守，又為漢陽太守。

translations.literal: When the sentence ended, he was summoned; after two transfers he was grand warden of Shanggu, again grand warden of Hanyang.
translations.idiomatic: After serving his sentence he was recalled, rose to grand warden of Shanggu, then again governed Hanyang.

sentences.zh: 時上邽令皇甫禎有臧罪，玄收考髡笞，死於冀巿，[二]一境皆震。

translations.literal: At the time the magistrate of Shanggui, Huangfu Zhen, had embezzlement crimes; Xuan seized him, examined him, shaved and flogged him, and he died in Ji market; [two] the whole territory was shaken.
translations.idiomatic: He flogged a corrupt magistrate to death in the marketplace and stunned the commandery.

sentences.zh: 郡人上邽姜岐，守道隱居，名聞西州。

translations.literal: A commandery man Jiang Qi of Shanggui kept the Way in reclusion; his name was heard across the western province.
translations.idiomatic: Jiang Qi of Shanggui was a famous recluse.

sentences.zh: 玄召以為吏，稱疾不就。

translations.literal: Xuan summoned him to be a clerk; he claimed illness and would not go.
translations.idiomatic: Qiao Xuan offered him a clerkship; Jiang pleaded illness.

sentences.zh: 玄怒，□督郵尹益逼致之，曰：「岐若不至，趣嫁其母。

translations.literal: Xuan was angry, (lacuna) ordered chief village inspector Yin Yi to force him, saying: "If Qi does not come, hasten to marry off his mother.
translations.idiomatic: Qiao Xuan ordered Yin Yi to drag Jiang in, threatening to marry off Jiang's mother if he refused.

sentences.zh: 」[三]益固爭不能得，遽曉譬岐。

translations.literal: " [Three] Yi firmly argued and could not prevail; he hastened to explain clearly to Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Yin Yi protested in vain, then begged Jiang to yield.

sentences.zh: 岐堅臥不起。

translations.literal: Qi lay firm and would not rise.
translations.idiomatic: Jiang Qi stayed in bed.
