sentences.zh: 郡內士大夫亦競往諫，玄乃止。

translations.literal: Gentry of the commandery also vied to go remonstrate; Xuan then stopped.
translations.idiomatic: Local scholars intervened and Qiao Xuan dropped the demand.

sentences.zh: 時頗以為譏。

translations.literal: At the time many took it as a subject of ridicule.
translations.idiomatic: People mocked Qiao Xuan for it.

sentences.zh: 後謝病免，復公車征為司徒長史，拜將作大匠。

translations.literal: Later he claimed illness and was dismissed; again summoned by official carriage as senior clerk of the minister of education, appointed superintendent of works.
translations.idiomatic: He retired ill, then returned as senior clerk of the minister of education and superintendent of works.

sentences.zh: 注[一]左部尉也。

translations.literal: Comment one: It means captain of the left section.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: Referring to the Luoyang left captaincy.

sentences.zh: 注[二]冀，縣名，屬漢陽郡。

translations.literal: Comment two: Ji is a county name belonging to Hanyang commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Note 2: Ji County was in Hanyang.

sentences.zh: 注[三]趣音促。

translations.literal: Comment three: Qu is read like cu.
translations.idiomatic: Note 3: Qu here means to hasten, read like cu.

sentences.zh: 桓帝末，鮮卑、南匈奴及高句驪嗣子伯固並畔，為寇鈔，四府舉玄為度遼將軍，假黃鉞。

translations.literal: At the end of Emperor Huan's reign the Xianbei, southern Xiongnu, and Gaogouli heir Bogu together rebelled, raiding; the four offices recommended Xuan as General Who Crosses the Liao, with yellow axe credentials.
translations.idiomatic: Late in Huan's reign the Xianbei, Xiongnu, and Koguryŏ raided; the four highest bureaus named Qiao Xuan general with ceremonial axe.

sentences.zh: 玄至鎮，休兵養士，然後督諸將守討擊胡虜及伯固等，皆破散退走。

translations.literal: When Xuan reached command, he rested troops and nurtured soldiers, then supervised the various generals in defense and strike against Hu bandits and Bogu and the like; all were broken and scattered and withdrew.
translations.idiomatic: He rested the army, then struck and routed the invaders.

sentences.zh: 在職三年，邊境安靜。

translations.literal: In office three years, the border was quiet.
translations.idiomatic: The frontier stayed calm for three years.

sentences.zh: 玄少子十歲，獨遊門次，卒有三人持杖劫執之，入捨登樓，就玄求貨，玄不與。

translations.literal: Xuan's youngest son, ten years old, played alone before the gate; suddenly three men with staves seized him, entered the house and climbed the tower, demanding goods from Xuan; Xuan would not give.
translations.idiomatic: Bandits kidnapped his ten-year-old son and demanded ransom; he refused.

sentences.zh: 有頃，司隸校尉陽球率河南尹、洛陽令圍守玄家。

translations.literal: After a while the colonel director of retainers Yang Qiu led the Henan governor and Luoyang magistrate to surround and guard Xuan's house.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Qiu surrounded the house with capital police.

sentences.zh: 球等恐並殺其子，未欲迫之。

translations.literal: Qiu and the others feared they would all kill his son and did not wish to press.
translations.idiomatic: Police hesitated lest a storming get the boy killed.

sentences.zh: 玄瞋目呼曰：「奸人無狀，玄豈以一子之命而縱國賊乎！

translations.literal: Xuan glared and shouted: "Wicked men without shame—would Xuan loose a state bandit for one son's life!
translations.idiomatic: Qiao Xuan roared that he would not ransom the realm to save one child.

sentences.zh: 」促令兵進。

translations.literal: " He urged the soldiers forward.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the assault.

sentences.zh: 於是攻之，玄子亦死。

translations.literal: Thereupon they attacked; Xuan's son also died.
translations.idiomatic: The bandits killed the boy in the fight.

sentences.zh: 玄乃詣闕謝罪，乞下天下：「凡有劫質，皆並殺之，不得贖以財寶，開張奸路。

translations.literal: Xuan then went to the palace gate to confess guilt, begging to publish under heaven: "Whenever there is seizure of hostages, all shall be killed together; they may not be ransomed with treasure goods, opening wide the wicked path.
translations.idiomatic: He asked the throne to decree that hostage-takers never be ransomed.

sentences.zh: 」詔書下其章。

translations.literal: " An edict promulgated his memorial.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor issued his proposal as law.

sentences.zh: 初自安帝以後，法禁稍□，京師劫質，不避豪貴，自是遂絕。

translations.literal: From the time after Emperor An, law prohibitions gradually slackened; capital hostage-taking did not avoid noble and great; from this it then ceased.
translations.idiomatic: After his policy, Luoyang kidnappings for ransom—once aimed even at magnates—largely ended.

sentences.zh: 玄以光和六年卒，時年七十五。

translations.literal: Xuan died in the sixth year of Guanghe at age seventy-five.
translations.idiomatic: He died in 183 CE at seventy-five.

sentences.zh: 玄性剛急無大體，然謙儉下士，子弟親宗無在大官者。

translations.literal: Xuan by nature was harsh and hasty without large pattern, yet he was modest and frugal toward gentlemen, and no sons, nephews, or kin held high office.
translations.idiomatic: He was brusque in temper yet humble to talent; no kinsman held high rank.

sentences.zh: 及卒，家無居業，喪無所殯，當時稱之。

translations.literal: When he died, the family had no settled estate, the funeral had nowhere to bury; the age praised him.
translations.idiomatic: He died poor and was praised for it.

sentences.zh: 初，曹操微時，人莫知者，嘗往候玄，玄見而異焉，謂曰：「今天下將亂，安生民，者其在君乎！

translations.literal: Formerly, when Cao Cao was lowly, no one knew him; he once went to wait on Xuan; Xuan saw him and thought him strange, saying to him: "Now the realm is about to be thrown into chaos; who will secure the black-haired people—perhaps it is you!
translations.idiomatic: In obscurity Cao Cao visited Qiao Xuan, who prophesied that he would save the age.

sentences.zh: 」操常感其知己。

translations.literal: " Cao Cao always felt gratitude for that knowing him.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Cao never forgot that recognition.

sentences.zh: 及後經過玄墓，輒淒愴致祭。

translations.literal: Later whenever passing Xuan's tomb, he always mourned and offered sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever he passed the grave he wept and sacrificed.

sentences.zh: 自為其文曰：

translations.literal: He himself composed the text saying:
translations.idiomatic: He drafted this elegy himself:

sentences.zh: 「故太尉橋公，懿德高軌，泛愛博容。

translations.literal: "The late grand commandant Lord Qiao, virtuous conduct high track, broad love wide tolerance.
translations.idiomatic: The late Grand Commandant Qiao: deep virtue, generous spirit.

sentences.zh: 國念明訓，士思令謨。

translations.literal: The state recalls bright instruction, gentlemen ponder fine plans.
translations.idiomatic: The state remembers his teaching; scholars recall his counsel.

sentences.zh: 幽靈潛翳，□哉緬矣！

translations.literal: Hidden spirit submerged in shade, alas how remote!
translations.idiomatic: His shade is hidden—how distant now!

sentences.zh: 操以幼年，逮升堂室，特以頑質，見納君子。

translations.literal: Cao in youth reached your hall chamber; by special favor, though stubborn in substance, he was received by the gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: I was a coarse youth yet you welcomed me in.

sentences.zh: 增榮益觀，皆由□助，猶仲尼稱不如顏淵，[一]李生厚歎賈復。

translations.literal: Increased glory added to my repute all came from your aid—still as Zhongni said he was not equal to Yan Yuan; [one] Master Li deeply sighed over Jia Fu.
translations.idiomatic: You raised me as Confucius praised Yan Hui and Li Sheng praised Jia Fu.

sentences.zh: [二]士死知己，懷此無忘。

translations.literal: [Two] A gentleman dies for one who knows him; cherishing this I do not forget.
translations.idiomatic: A man may die for his patron; I have not forgotten.

sentences.zh: 又承從容約誓之言：『徂沒之後，路有經由，不以鬥酒隻雞過相沃酹，車過三步，腹痛勿怨。

translations.literal: Further I received your relaxed covenant words: "After we are gone, if the road passes by, do not fail to pour a libation with a single cup of wine and one chicken; if the chariot passes three paces, do not blame bellyache.
translations.idiomatic: You joked that after death I should toast your grave with cheap wine or you would give me a bellyache from beyond.

sentences.zh: 』雖臨時戲笑之言，非至親之篤好，胡肯為此辭哉？

translations.literal: " Though temporary jesting words, unless the utmost kinship of good friendship, how would one make such phrases?
translations.idiomatic: Only the closest friends would swear such a jest.

sentences.zh: 懷舊惟顧，念之淒愴。

translations.literal: Thinking of old times, looking back, pondering it is mournful.
translations.idiomatic: Remembering you still brings grief.

sentences.zh: [三] 奉命東征，屯次鄉里，北望貴土，乃心陵墓。

translations.literal: [Three] Ordered on eastern campaign, halting at my village, gazing north at your noble soil, my heart is toward the tomb mound.
translations.idiomatic: On campaign I halt near home and turn my heart to your tomb.

sentences.zh: 裁致薄奠，公其享之！

translations.literal: I send only a thin offering; may you partake!
translations.idiomatic: Accept this meager offering.

sentences.zh: 」[四] 注[一]論語孔子謂子貢曰：「汝與回也孰愈？

translations.literal: " [Four] Comment one: The Analects: Confucius said to Zigong, "You and Hui—who is better?
translations.idiomatic: Note 4: Confucius compared Zigong with Yan Hui.

sentences.zh: 」子貢曰：「賜也何敢望回。

translations.literal: " Zigong said: "How dare Si compare to Hui.
translations.idiomatic: Zigong disclaimed comparison.

sentences.zh: 」子曰：「吾與汝俱不如也。」

translations.literal: " The Master said: "I with you both am not equal.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said neither matched Yan Hui.

sentences.zh: 注[二]復少好學，師事舞陰李生。

translations.literal: Comment two: Fu in youth loved learning, studied with Master Li of Wuyin.
translations.idiomatic: Note 2: Jia Fu studied under Li Sheng.

sentences.zh: 李生奇之，曰：「賈君國器也。」

translations.literal: Master Li marveled at him, saying: "Lord Jia is the state's vessel.
translations.idiomatic: Li Sheng called Jia Fu a vessel of state.

sentences.zh: 注[三]惟，思也。

translations.literal: Comment three: Wei means to think.
translations.idiomatic: Note 3: Wei means to ponder.

sentences.zh: 注[四]魏志曰「建安七年，曹公軍譙，遂至浚儀，遣使以太牢祀橋玄，進軍官度」也。

translations.literal: Comment four: The Wei Records says "In the seventh year of Jian'an, Lord Cao's army was at Qiao, then reached Junyi, sent envoys with grand sacrifice to Qiao Xuan, advanced the army to Guandu.
translations.idiomatic: Note 4: The Wei zhi records Cao Cao's sacrifice at Qiao Xuan's tomb in 202 CE.

sentences.zh: 玄子羽，官至任城相。

translations.literal: Xuan's son Yu reached office as chancellor of Rencheng.
translations.idiomatic: His son Qiao Yu became chancellor of Rencheng.

sentences.zh: 論曰：任棠、姜岐，世著其清。

translations.literal: The essay says: Ren Tang and Jiang Qi, generations record their purity.
translations.idiomatic: The historian praises Ren Tang and Jiang Qi for purity.

sentences.zh: 結甕牖而辭三命，[一]殆漢陽之幽人乎？

translations.literal: Tying a jar window and declining three summonses; [one] are they nearly the recluses of Hanyang?
translations.idiomatic: They lived in poverty and refused office thrice—true Hanyang recluses?

sentences.zh: [二] 龐參躬求賢之禮，故民悅其政；

translations.literal: [Two] Pang Shen personally sought worthy ritual, therefore the people rejoiced in his government;
translations.idiomatic: Pang Shen humbled himself to worthies and won the people.

sentences.zh: 橋玄厲邦君之威，而觿失其情。

translations.literal: Qiao Xuan displayed a lord's awe, yet the many lost their proper feeling.
translations.idiomatic: Qiao Xuan threw his weight around and alienated opinion.

sentences.zh: 夫豈力不足歟？

translations.literal: Was it that strength was insufficient?
translations.idiomatic: Was it mere weakness?

sentences.zh: 將有道在焉。

translations.literal: There must be a Way in it.
translations.idiomatic: The Way of governance was at stake.

sentences.zh: [三]如令其道可忘，則強梁勝矣。

translations.literal: [Three] If one could forget that Way, then the violent would win.
translations.idiomatic: Forget the Way and bullies prevail.

sentences.zh: 語曰：「三軍可奪帥，匹夫不可奪志。

translations.literal: The saying goes: "An army may seize its general; a common man may not seize his will.
translations.idiomatic: The Analects says an army cannot crush a resolute commoner's will.

sentences.zh: 」[四]子貢曰：「寧喪千金，不失士心。

translations.literal: " Zigong said: "Better lose a thousand in gold than lose gentlemen's hearts.
translations.idiomatic: Zigong said better lose gold than lose the hearts of good men.

sentences.zh: 」昔段干木踰牆而避文侯之命，[五]洩柳閉門不納穆公之請。

translations.literal: " Formerly Duan Ganmu climbed the wall to avoid Marquis Wen of Wei's command; [five] Xie Liu closed the gate and would not admit Duke Mu of Lu's request.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Ganmu fled Marquis Wen; Xie Liu barred Duke Mu.

sentences.zh: [六]貴必有所屈，賤亦有所申矣。

translations.literal: [Six] The noble must sometimes bend; the lowly also sometimes extend.
translations.idiomatic: High rank must yield to principle; humble men may assert it.

sentences.zh: 注[一]結猶構也。

translations.literal: Comment one: Jie means to build.
translations.idiomatic: Note 1: Jie means to frame or build.

sentences.zh: 莊子曰：「原憲處魯，居環堵之室，桑樞而甕牖。

translations.literal: Zhuangzi says: "Yuan Xian dwelt in Lu, lived in a ring of dunned walls, mulberry pivot and jar window.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuangzi pictures Yuan Xian's poverty.

sentences.zh: 」周禮：「一命受職，再命受服，三命受位。

translations.literal: " The Zhou li: "One summons receives office, two summons receive dress, three summons receive rank.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou li describes graded summons to office.

sentences.zh: 」謂任、姜辭太守之辟也。

translations.literal: It means Ren and Jiang declined the grand warden's summons.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Tang and Jiang Qi refused the grand warden.

sentences.zh: 注[二]易曰：「履道坦坦，幽人貞吉。」

translations.literal: Comment two: The Changes says: "Walking the Way smooth and smooth, the recluse is auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Note 2: The Zhou yi praises the recluse's constancy.

sentences.zh: 注[三]橋玄之捨姜岐，以道不可違，故不得以威力逼也。

translations.literal: Comment three: Qiao Xuan's releasing Jiang Qi—because the Way could not be violated, therefore he could not use might to force.
translations.idiomatic: Note 3: Qiao Xuan yielded to Jiang Qi because the Way forbade coercion.

sentences.zh: 注[四]鄭玄注論語云：「匹夫之守志，重於三軍之死將者也。」

translations.literal: Comment four: Zheng Xuan's commentary on the Analects says: "A common man's guarding his will weighs heavier than an army's losing its general.
translations.idiomatic: Note 4: Zheng Xuan glosses the will as paramount.

sentences.zh: 注[五]高士傳曰，段干木者，晉人也。

translations.literal: Comment five: The Lives of High Men says Duan Ganmu was a man of Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Note 5: Duan Ganmu of Jin fled power.

sentences.zh: 守道不仕。

translations.literal: He kept the Way and did not serve.
translations.idiomatic: He refused office.

sentences.zh: 魏文侯造其門，段干木踰牆而避之。

translations.literal: Marquis Wen of Wei visited his gate; Duan Ganmu climbed the wall to avoid him.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Wen found him gone over the wall.

sentences.zh: 注[六]洩柳，魯之賢人也。

translations.literal: Comment six: Xie Liu was a worthy man of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Note 6: Xie Liu was Lu's recluse.

sentences.zh: 魯穆公時，請見之，洩柳閉門而不納。

translations.literal: In Duke Mu of Lu's time he requested an audience; Xie Liu closed the gate and would not admit.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Mu could not force an interview.

sentences.zh: 事見孟子。

translations.literal: The affair is seen in the Mencius.
translations.idiomatic: The Mencius records it.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：李叟勤身，甘饑辭饋。

translations.literal: The encomium says: The old man Li toiled his body, sweet on hunger, declining gifts.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xun lived lean, refusing charity.

sentences.zh: 禪為君隱，之死靡貳。

translations.literal: Chan concealed for the lord, to death without second thoughts.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Chan shielded his prince unto death.

sentences.zh: 龜習邊功，參起徒中。

translations.literal: Gui practiced border merit; Shen rose from among convicts.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Gui knew the frontier; Pang Shen rose from chains.

sentences.zh: 橋公識運，先覺時雄。

translations.literal: Lord Qiao read fate's turn, first aware of the coming hero.
translations.idiomatic: Qiao Xuan spotted the hero before the world did.

sentences.zh: 校勘記

translations.literal: Collation notes
translations.idiomatic: Textual collation

sentences.zh: 一六八四頁一０行州辟治中從事按：集解引錢大昕說，謂章懷避唐諱，凡「治」字或改為「理」，或改為「化」，或改為「持」，此「治中」字亦必改易，宋人校書者又回改耳。

translations.literal: Page 1684 line 10: province summoned administrative adjutant. Note: Collected glosses cite Qian Daxin: Zhanghuai avoided Tang taboo, every graph zhi sometimes changed to li, hua, or chi; this administrative adjutant graph must also have been altered, Song collators reverted.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Tang taboos corrupted zhi to chi in adjutant titles; Song editors restored.

sentences.zh: 一六八四頁一三行夷賊素聞其聲按：汲本、殿本「聲」上有「名」字。

translations.literal: Page 1684 line 13: barbarian bandits long heard his name. Note: Ji and Palace editions have the graph for name above sound.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Some editions add the word name before reputation.

sentences.zh: 一六八五頁七行韎任朱離按：集解引錢大昕說，謂此句上下當有脫文，未必詩有此語。

translations.literal: Page 1685 line 7: Mei Ren Zhuli. Note: Collected glosses cite Qian Daxin: this sentence above and below ought to have lacunae; not necessarily the Odes had this phrase.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Qian Daxin doubts the musical gloss is complete.

sentences.zh: 一六八五頁一五行手足異處刊誤謂「手」當作「首」。

translations.literal: Page 1685 line 15: hands and feet in different places; the Corrections says hand should read head.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Some argue for decapitation rather than dismemberment.

sentences.zh: 今按：史記孔子世家亦作「手足異處」，惟谷梁傳作「首足異門而出」，劉氏殆據谷梁傳言也。

translations.literal: Now checked: Records of the Grand Historian Confucius's house also writes hands and feet in different places; only Guliang writes head and foot out different gates; Liu probably followed Guliang.
translations.idiomatic: The Shi ji agrees on limbs; Guliang differs.

sentences.zh: 一六八六頁三行毛詩無韎任朱離之文按：集解引黃山說，謂賢注引薛君韓詩說，不及「韎任朱離」，是韓詩亦無此句，不獨毛詩也。

translations.literal: Page 1686 line 3: Mao Odes lacks Mei Ren Zhuli text. Note: Collected glosses cite Huang Shan: the worthy commentary cites Xue Jun's Han Odes explanation and does not reach Mei Ren Zhuli; Han Odes also lacks this sentence, not only Mao.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Huang Shan argues Han Odes lacks the line too.

sentences.zh: 今曰毛詩無，「毛」字當為後人妄改。

translations.literal: Now saying Mao Odes lacks—the graph Mao must be a later person's reckless change.
translations.idiomatic: The word Mao may be a later scribal error.

sentences.zh: 注不及毛傳，必不捨韓而計毛也。

translations.literal: The commentary does not reach Mao commentary; it must not abandon Han and count Mao.
translations.idiomatic: The note follows Han learning, not Mao alone.

sentences.zh: 一六八六頁六行縣度者山名也按：前書西域傳「山名也」作「石山也」，此鬥。

translations.literal: Page 1686 line 6: Suspension Crossing is a mountain name. Note: Former Documents Western Regions treatise "mountain name" writes "stone mountain"; this is a corruption.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Han shu reads stone mountain.

sentences.zh: 章帝紀注引作「石山也」，不鬥。

translations.literal: Emperor Zhang's annals commentary citation writes stone mountain; not corrupted.
translations.idiomatic: Zhanghuai's other citation reads correctly.

sentences.zh: 一六八六頁六行去陽關五千八百八十里按：前書「八十里」作「八十八里」。

translations.literal: Page 1686 line 6: distance from Yang Pass five thousand eight hundred eighty li. Note: Former Documents "eighty li" reads "eighty-eight li.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Mileage variant eighty versus eighty-eight.

sentences.zh: 一六八九頁一０行始復得還都令居按：集解引黃山說，謂通鑒「都」作「治」，此避唐諱改。

translations.literal: Page 1689 line 10: first again able to return and garrison Lingju. Note: Collected glosses cite Huang Shan: Comprehensive Mirror writes govern for capital; this avoids Tang taboo change.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Zizhi tongjian reads zhi for du to avoid a Tang taboo.

sentences.zh: 一六九一頁一行*

translations.literal: Page 1691 line 1: lacuna marker.
translations.idiomatic: Lacuna in manuscript.

sentences.zh: (順帝時)

translations.literal: (during Emperor Shun)
translations.idiomatic: Parenthetical Shun era.

sentences.zh: *以為太尉沈欽韓謂上有永建元年事，此「順帝時」三字衍文。

translations.literal: and appointed him grand commandant—Shen Qinhan says because above there is Yongjian first year matter, these three graphs Shun era are redundant text.
translations.idiomatic: Shen Qinhan deletes redundant Shun-era gloss.

sentences.zh: 今據刪。

translations.literal: Now deleted accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: The redundant phrase is removed.

sentences.zh: 一六九一頁五行夫國以賢化集解引惠棟說，謂「化」當作「治」。

translations.literal: Page 1691 line 5: the state by worthies transforms. Collected glosses cite Hui Dong: hua should read zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Hui Dong restores zhi for Tang-taboo hua.

sentences.zh: 按：此亦章懷避諱改。

translations.literal: Note: this also is Zhanghuai taboo change.
translations.idiomatic: Another Tang taboo substitution.

sentences.zh: 一六九一頁九行言其來歸何刊誤謂「言其」當作「其言」。

translations.literal: Page 1691 line 9: why speak of his coming home; Corrections says speak his should read his speak.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Gongyang word order his speak.

sentences.zh: 按：今本公羊傳作「其言」。

translations.literal: Note: now Gongyang transmission writes his speak.
translations.idiomatic: Modern Gongyang texts agree.

sentences.zh: 一六九一頁一三行良字邵平按：集解引惠棟說，謂長沙耆舊傳作「字邵卿」，水經注亦作「邵卿」，章懷注誤。

translations.literal: Page 1691 line 13: Liang styled Shaoping. Note: Collected glosses cite Hui Dong: Changsha elders' tradition writes styled Shaoqing, Shuijing zhu also Shaoqing; Zhanghuai note errs.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Zhu Liang's courtesy name may be Shaoqing not Shaoping.

sentences.zh: 一六九二頁八行*

translations.literal: Page 1692 line 8: lacuna marker.
translations.idiomatic: Lacuna.

translations.literal: (to)
translations.idiomatic: Fragment to.

sentences.zh: *臣*[至]*頑駑據刊誤改。

translations.literal: your subject to stupid nags corrected per Corrections.
translations.idiomatic: Emended per Corrections.

sentences.zh: 一六九二頁一０行上籩聖*

translations.literal: Page 1692 line 10: above fouls sagely *
translations.idiomatic: Lacuna before court.

translations.literal: (court)
translations.idiomatic: Court supplied.

sentences.zh: **[明]*據汲本﹑殿本改。

translations.literal: **bright** per Ji and Palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Bright emended from Ji and Palace.

sentences.zh: 一六九四頁四行乃推授舜*[授舜]*則天下得其利而丹朱病刊誤謂案史記本文，更有「授舜」二字。

translations.literal: Page 1694 line 4: then pushed grant to Shun (lacuna grant Shun) then realm gained its benefit and Dan Zhu ill; Corrections says checking Records basic text, there are further two graphs grant Shun.
translations.idiomatic: Collation supplies missing grant Shun in Yao-Shun passage.

sentences.zh: 今據補。

translations.literal: Now supplemented accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: Text supplemented.

sentences.zh: 一六九五頁一行七世祖仁從同郡戴德學按：「戴德」當作「戴聖」。

translations.literal: Page 1695 line 1: seventh-generation ancestor Ren studied with Dai De of same commandery. Note: "Dai De" should read Dai Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: teacher should be Dai Sheng not Dai De.

sentences.zh: 集解引朱彝尊說，謂案前書儒林傳，仁傳小戴之學，此云「戴德」，恐誤。

translations.literal: Collected glosses cite Zhu Yizun: checking Former Documents Confucian forest biography, Ren transmitted lesser Dai learning; this saying Dai De, probably error.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Yizun confirms Lesser Dai lineage.

sentences.zh: 一六九五頁二行成帝時為大鴻臚按：集解引洪亮吉說，謂案前書百官表，平帝元始元年始雲大鴻臚橋仁，今言「成帝時」，誤。

translations.literal: Page 1695 line 2: in Emperor Cheng's time he was grand herald. Note: Collected glosses cite Hong Liangji: checking Former Documents hundred offices table, only Ping Emperor Yuan shi first year has grand herald Qiao Ren; now saying Emperor Cheng time, error.
translations.idiomatic: Hong Liangji dates Qiao Ren's grand herald office to Ping Di not Cheng Di.

sentences.zh: 一六九五頁三行陳相羊昌按：集解引何焯說，謂「羊」舊抄廣川書跋作「芊」。

translations.literal: Page 1695 line 3: Chancellor of Chen Yang Chang. Note: Collected glosses cite He Zhuo: sheep old manuscript Guangchuan calligraphy postscript writes qian graph.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: surname may be miswritten sheep for another graph.

sentences.zh: 一六九六頁一五行家無居業按：集解引惠棟說，謂張璠漢記「居業」作「余業」。

translations.literal: Page 1696 line 15: family had no settled estate. Note: Collected glosses cite Hui Dong: Zhang Fan Han ji writes surplus estate.
translations.idiomatic: Variant surplus for settled in Han records.

sentences.zh: 一六九七頁二行懿德高軌按：三國魏志注作「誕敷明德」。

translations.literal: Page 1697 line 2: virtuous conduct high track. Note: Three Kingdoms Wei Records commentary writes spread bright virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Wei zhi variant wording for epitaph.

sentences.zh: 一六九七頁三行幽靈潛翳□哉緬矣按：魏志注作「靈幽體翳，邈哉晞矣」。

translations.literal: Page 1697 line 3: hidden spirit submerged shade alas how remote. Note: Wei Records commentary writes spirit dark body hidden, remote alas dawn.
translations.idiomatic: Wei zhi commentary variant for elegy line.

sentences.zh: 一六九七頁三行特以頑質見納君子按：魏志注作「特以頑鄙之姿，為大君子所納」。

translations.literal: Page 1697 line 3: by special favor stubborn substance received by gentleman. Note: Wei Records commentary writes by special favor coarse mean substance, by great gentleman received.
translations.idiomatic: Wei zhi fuller phrasing.

sentences.zh: 一六九七頁四行皆由□助按：魏志注同，汲本﹑殿本「助」作「勖」。

translations.literal: Page 1697 line 4: all came from your aid lacuna. Note: Wei Records commentary same; Ji and Palace editions write encourage for aid.
translations.idiomatic: Ji and Palace read encourage instead of aid.

sentences.zh: 一六九七頁五行徂沒之後按：魏志注「沒」作「逝」。

translations.literal: Page 1697 line 5: after we are gone. Note: Wei Records commentary writes pass for gone.
translations.idiomatic: Wei zhi uses pass for gone.

sentences.zh: 一六九七頁五行腹痛勿怨按：魏志注「怨」作「怪」。

translations.literal: Page 1697 line 5: bellyache do not blame. Note: Wei Records commentary writes blame for strange.
translations.idiomatic: Wei zhi reads strange instead of blame.
